addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.
7	
8	## Current Design
9	
10	The architectural backbone lives in BUS0 §Remote Node Access (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`)
11	plus three platform subdocs (`BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc`, `BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc`,
12	`BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc`). Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock
13	captures only state that doesn't fit there.
14	
15	**Header decisions made in this heat:**
16	
17	- BURH regime renamed to BURN (n = Node) — decision; execution in AAV/AAW
18	- BURN_TIER field added: `privileged | workload`
19	- Two-tier model:
20	  - Privileged tier: node-level ops (sshd config, user provisioning).
21	    Multi-resident allowed; one operator at a time by social contract.
22	  - Workload tier: user-level ops (normal remote work). Exclusive to one
23	    station user; ephemeral.
24	- Verb vocabulary (BUSN quoins): Garrison / Conscript / Discharge / Inventory
25	- Tabtarget colophon families under `buw-`:
26	  - `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops (list / render / validate)
27	  - `buw-rnc` — SSH config aggregator (cross-tier, all profiles)
28	  - `buw-npg{c,w,p}` — Garrison per Windows shell (Cygwin/WSL/PowerShell)
29	  - `buw-nwc{c,w,p}` — Conscript per Windows shell
30	  - `buw-nw{d,i}` — Discharge / Inventory (Windows-side)
31	  - `buw-nw{k,r,s}` — Knock / Run / Ssh (workload operational)
32	  - `buw-hn0` — Handbook landing for node ops
33	- Workload responsiveness "knock" colophon is `buw-nwk` (originally
34	  `buw-nwc`; renamed to remove visual collision with conscript family
35	  `buw-nwc{platform}`).
36	- Linux/Mac/Localhost garrison and conscript variants (`buw-npg{l,m,x}`,
37	  `buw-nwc{l,m,x}`) are not slated and not itched. Mint as new paces when
38	  a first non-Windows fundus target actually arrives. Legacy `buw-rhc{l,m,x}`
39	  (BURN-renamed in AAV) survives as transitional code; localhost remains
40	  exercised by AAc and AAP regression paces.
41	- AAQ (BUSNW elaboration) dropped: BUS0 backbone is sufficient spec; per-
42	  platform contracts crystallize through implementation paces and AAC
43	  practice. BUSNW stub (36 lines) stands as-is.
44	
45	## Heat Sequence
46	
47	**Implementation phase (sequenced):**
48	
49	- ₢A-AAV burh-to-burn-rename-buk — mechanical rename + tier-refusal helper
50	  + SSH config aggregator move (`buw-HWsc` → `buw-rnc`)
51	- ₢A-AAW burh-to-burn-rename-jjk — mechanical, depends on AAV
52	- ₢A-AAX garrison-windows-machinery — three Windows shells; defines key-
53	  line wire format (`# BURN:<alias>` marker) inline; updates BUS0 catalog
54	- ₢A-AAY conscript-discharge-inventory-windows — Windows workload-tier
55	  mirror, depends on AAX; updates BUS0 catalog
56	- ₢A-AAZ workload-operational-tabtargets — Check / Run / Ssh
57	- ₢A-AAa node-handbook-landing-and-windows-residue — handbook restructure,
58	  depends on AAX and AAY
59	
60	**Curia smoke (FIRST testing — runs on Mac before any Windows trip):**
61	
62	- ₢A-AAc localhost-smoke-pre-windows — fundus scenario test on Mac;
63	  validates BURN rename + Windows-only scope didn't break localhost path
64	
65	**Practice phase (Windows host):**
66	
67	- ₢A-AAC practice-access-procedures — Garrison/Conscript on real Windows;
68	  pass criteria specified per shell variant in pace docket
69	- ₢A-AAD practice-environment-procedures — WSL + Cygwin install
70	- ₢A-AAE practice-fundus-provisioning — JJK fundus inside WSL

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.

## Current Design

The architectural backbone lives in BUS0 §Remote Node Access (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`)
plus three platform subdocs (`BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc`, `BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc`,
`BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc`). Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock
captures only state that doesn't fit there.

**Header decisions made in this heat:**

- BURH regime renamed to BURN (n = Node) — decision; execution in AAV/AAW
- BURN_TIER field added: `privileged | workload`
- Two-tier model:
  - Privileged tier: node-level ops (sshd config, user provisioning).
    Multi-resident allowed; one operator at a time by social contract.
  - Workload tier: user-level ops (normal remote work). Exclusive to one
    station user; ephemeral.
- Verb vocabulary (BUSN quoins): Garrison / Conscript / Discharge / Inventory
- Tabtarget colophon families under `buw-`:
  - `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops (list / render / validate)
  - `buw-rnc` — SSH config aggregator (cross-tier, all profiles)
  - `buw-npg{c,w,p}` — Garrison per Windows shell (Cygwin/WSL/PowerShell)
  - `buw-nwc{c,w,p}` — Conscript per Windows shell
  - `buw-nw{d,i}` — Discharge / Inventory (Windows-side)
  - `buw-nw{k,r,s}` — Knock / Run / Ssh (workload operational)
  - `buw-hn0` — Handbook landing for node ops
- Workload responsiveness "knock" colophon is `buw-nwk` (originally
  `buw-nwc`; renamed to remove visual collision with conscript family
  `buw-nwc{platform}`).
- Linux/Mac/Localhost garrison and conscript variants (`buw-npg{l,m,x}`,
  `buw-nwc{l,m,x}`) are not slated and not itched. Mint as new paces when
  a first non-Windows fundus target actually arrives. Legacy `buw-rhc{l,m,x}`
  (BURN-renamed in AAV) survives as transitional code; localhost remains
  exercised by AAc and AAP regression paces.
- AAQ (BUSNW elaboration) dropped: BUS0 backbone is sufficient spec; per-
  platform contracts crystallize through implementation paces and AAC
  practice. BUSNW stub (36 lines) stands as-is.

## Heat Sequence

**Implementation phase (sequenced):**

- ₢A-AAV burh-to-burn-rename-buk — mechanical rename + tier-refusal helper
  + SSH config aggregator move (`buw-HWsc` → `buw-rnc`)
- ₢A-AAW burh-to-burn-rename-jjk — mechanical, depends on AAV
- ₢A-AAX garrison-windows-machinery — three Windows shells; defines key-
  line wire format (`# BURN:<alias>` marker) inline; updates BUS0 catalog
- ₢A-AAY conscript-discharge-inventory-windows — Windows workload-tier
  mirror, depends on AAX; updates BUS0 catalog
- ₢A-AAZ workload-operational-tabtargets — Check / Run / Ssh
- ₢A-AAa node-handbook-landing-and-windows-residue — handbook restructure,
  depends on AAX and AAY

**Curia smoke (FIRST testing — runs on Mac before any Windows trip):**

- ₢A-AAc localhost-smoke-pre-windows — fundus scenario test on Mac;
  validates BURN rename + Windows-only scope didn't break localhost path

**Practice phase (Windows host):**

- ₢A-AAC practice-access-procedures — Garrison/Conscript on real Windows;
  pass criteria specified per shell variant in pace docket
- ₢A-AAD practice-environment-procedures — WSL + Cygwin install
- ₢A-AAE practice-fundus-provisioning — JJK fundus inside WSL
- ₢A-AAF practice-docker-procedures — Docker dual-daemon

**Final regression:**

- ₢A-AAP retest-fundus-after-practice — re-run localhost fundus scenario
  test after Windows practice has potentially amended code

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged
  trust. Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their
  own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin
  scenarios. Pairs naturally with Adopt when both are needed.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't
  duplicate.

## Standing Notes

- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as
  `100.x.y.z` / tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency
- Windows username `b hyslop` (with space) — audit shell quoting at
  every `${BURN_USER}` expansion during practice
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is
  the Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call
- BURS_USER station regime field routes profiles to
  `.buk/users/${BURS_USER}/` subdirectory
- Three Windows BURN profiles for `rocket` distinguished by
  `BURN_COMMAND`: cygwin (`...bash.exe -l`), WSL (`wsl.exe -d ...`),
  PowerShell (`powershell.exe`)

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAd

## Character

Mechanical authoring against settled spec — 15 small subdocs following one shape. Pattern-replication, not design.

## Goal

One detail-site subdoc per BURN-domain tabtarget quoin (Windows-relevant scope, 15 subdocs). Each lives at `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSTxx-<silks>.adoc` and contains an `axhob_operation` block elaborating parameters, preconditions, and guarantees against the corresponding `bust_*` voicing in BUS0.

## Constraints

- Reuse the procedure/method hierarchy markers per AXLA `axvo_tabtarget` allowance — no new markers minted at the detail site.
- Introduces `BUST` as a parent prefix in the doc namespace, parallel to existing `BUSN`. Two-letter `xx` mnemonic per tabtarget decided at mount time; `BUST` itself is not a name.
- BUS0 includes each new subdoc. The 21 voicings in BUS0's `=== Tabtarget Catalog` stay unchanged; only `include::` lines are added.
- No code changes. No tabtarget filename changes.

## Scope

15 quoins drawn from BUS0's `// Concrete BURN Tabtargets` mapping block — every `bust_*` quoin except the six Linux/Mac/Localhost garrison/conscript variants. Discovery: `grep '^:bust_' Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc` then exclude `_linux`, `_mac`, `_localhost`.

The Linux/Mac/Localhost garrison/conscript subdocs are explicitly deferred per the paddock's non-Windows-deferral note; they belong to the future paces that introduce non-Windows fundus implementations.

## Done

All 15 subdocs exist under `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/`, all are `include::`'d from BUS0, the existing 21 voicings in BUS0's catalog remain unchanged, and `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` passes.

content.text:
Mounted. Heat is **rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures** (₣A-, 8/21 done, racing).

**Next pace**: `₢A-AAd node-tabtarget-subdocs` [rough]

**Character**: Mechanical pattern-replication, not design.

**Goal in plain terms**: For each of the 15 Windows-relevant BURN tabtarget quoins voiced in BUS0, create a detail-site subdoc at `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSTxx-<silks>.adoc` containing an `axhob_operation` block. Wire each into BUS0 with `include::`. The 21 voicings in BUS0's catalog stay untouched.

**Recent context**: 
- f650d45f minted `axvo_tabtarget`/`axd_imprint` and converted BUS0 catalog to per-tabtarget voicings; `buw-nwc operational` was renamed to `buw-nwk knock`
- e603b10d/b7aa388f/03ca4355 paddock currying around AAd's predecessors

**Name assessment**: "node-tabtarget-subdocs" — fits the work (it's the subdoc layer for the BURN/node tabtarget catalog). I'd keep it.

**Proposed approach**:
1. Discovery — `grep '^:bust_' Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`, exclude `_linux|_mac|_localhost`, confirm count = 15
2. Read one existing parallel subdoc (BUSNW or similar) and the `axvo_tabtarget` voicing shape in AXLA to nail the `axhob_operation` template
3. Mint the 15 two-letter `xx` mnemonics in one pass against the 15 names — mnemonic table for review before generation
4. Generate the 15 subdocs from one shared template, filling per-quoin parameters/preconditions/guarantees pulled from BUS0 voicings
5. Add 15 `include::` lines to BUS0; run `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh`

**Execution strategy**: Sequential sonnet. Mechanical, but mnemonic minting and per-quoin parameter extraction need careful reading of BUS0 — not parallelizable without losing consistency. The 15 subdocs share a template; I'll draft once and instantiate.

Step 2 (read a parallel subdoc + AXLA voicing) is the tightest unknown — the docket says "reuse procedure/method hierarchy markers per `axvo_tabtarget` allowance" but I want to see the existing shape before committing to a template. Want me to proceed, or pause after Step 2 with the template + mnemonic table for review?

2460	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}

2462	
2463	=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2464	
2465	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2466	|===
2467	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
2468	
2469	|{axhob_operation}          |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The operation this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
2470	|{axhoc_completion}         |0                            |Introduces the signaling contract section (exit codes, status); bare marker, no lookahead
2471	|{axhopt_typed_parameter}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (zero new terms minted)
2472	|{axhopa_arg_parameter}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses a shared CLI argument term (small vocabulary per spec)
2473	|{axhopm_motif_parameter}   |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
2474	|{axhoot_typed_output}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named output whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (parallel to typed_parameter pattern)
2475	|{axhoom_motif_output}      |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
2476	|{axhoq_precondition}       |0                            |A required condition before execution; bare marker, no lookahead
2477	|{axhog_guarantee}          |0                            |A promised condition after execution; bare marker, no lookahead
2478	|{axhos_step}               |0                            |A named behavioral step; bare marker, no lookahead
2479	|{axhos_waymark}            |0                            |A branch-targetable step with a local AsciiDoc anchor; bare marker, no lookahead
2480	|===
2481	
2482	[[axhob_operation]]
2483	{axhob_operation}::
2484	Opens the operation hierarchy block at the
2485	{axl_detail_site}.
2486	Lookahead: 1
2487	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2488	— the operation this detail site describes.
2489	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
2490	{axvo_procedure},
2491	{axvo_method},
2492	or
2493	{axvo_tabtarget}
2494	at the
2495	{axl_definition_site}.
2496	No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions are authoritative at the
2497	{axl_definition_site}
2498	only.
2499	
2500	The surface syntax:
2501	
2502	[source,asciidoc]
2503	----
2504	//axhob_operation
2505	{rbtgo_depot_levy}
2506	
2507	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2508	{rbtgo_depot_levy_name}
2509	----
2510	
2511	[[axhoc_completion]]
2512	{axhoc_completion}::
2513	Marks the signaling contract section in an operation
2514	{axl_detail_site}.
2515	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; content follows directly.
2516	Documents how the operation communicates success or failure: exit codes for CLI, HTTP status for REST, return values for functions.
2517	Distinct from
2518	{axhog_guarantee}
2519	which describes state promises, and
2520	{axhoot_typed_output}
2521	/{axhoom_motif_output}
2522	which describe produced data.
2523	One per operation.
2524	
2525	[[axhopt_typed_parameter]]
2526	{axhopt_typed_parameter}::
2527	Marks a named input parameter in an operation
2528	{axl_detail_site}
2529	whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
2530	No new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
2531	Used when the parameter's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., a parameter that is simply a CIDR block or a domain name).
2532	Lookahead: 1
2533	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2534	— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.

2536

2539	Marks a named input parameter in an operation
2540	{axl_detail_site}
2541	whose lookahead reuses a shared CLI argument term.
2542	The attribute reference on the following line names a shared argument from the small CLI argument vocabulary defined per spec; no new term is minted.
2543	Used when the parameter is a well-known CLI argument that appears across multiple operations.
2544	Lookahead: 1
2545	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2546	— must resolve to an existing shared CLI argument anchor; small vocabulary per spec.
2547	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2548	
2549	[[axhopm_motif_parameter]]

2551	Marks an input parameter in an operation
2552	{axl_detail_site}
2553	whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
2554	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
2555	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2556	
2557	[[axhoot_typed_output]]
2558	{axhoot_typed_output}::
2559	Marks a named output in an operation
2560	{axl_detail_site}
2561	whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
2562	Parallel to the

2568	— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
2569	Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
2570	
2571	[[axhoom_motif_output]]
2572	{axhoom_motif_output}::
2573	Marks an output in an operation
2574	{axl_detail_site}
2575	whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
2576	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
2577	Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
2578	+
2579	May carry
2580	{axd_fact}
2581	to indicate the output is delivered as a named fact file, or
2582	{axd_tally}
2583	to indicate a fact map of dynamically-named files sharing a common infix.
2584	
2585	[[axhoq_precondition]]
2586	{axhoq_precondition}::
2587	Marks a required precondition in an operation
2588	{axl_detail_site}.
2589	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2590	Each precondition is a statement about required environmental state before execution.
2591	
2592	[[axhog_guarantee]]
2593	{axhog_guarantee}::
2594	Marks a postcondition guarantee in an operation
2595	{axl_detail_site}.
2596	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2597	Each guarantee is a promise about environmental state after successful execution.
2598	
2599	[[axhos_step]]
2600	{axhos_step}::
2601	Marks a named behavioral step in an operation
2602	{axl_detail_site}.
2603	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2604	Steps carry their identity through position and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
2605	Branching between steps is expressed in prose using cross-references or by directing to a
2606	{axhos_waymark}
2607	anchor.
2608	
2609	[[axhos_waymark]]
2610	{axhos_waymark}::
2611	Variant of
2612	{axhos_step}
2613	for steps that must be branch-targetable from other steps.
2614	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2615	Rare; used when prose in another step says "go to step X" and needs a concrete anchor target.
2616	Carries a local AsciiDoc anchor following the `operation_waymarkname` naming convention (e.g., `[[depot_levy_retry]]`).
2617	The anchor enables intra-document cross-references without S0 elevation — the step remains at the detail site level.
2618	
2619	=== Nesting Rules
2620	
2621	Operation hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
2622	
2623	* {axhob_operation}
2624	opens the operation block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
2625	{axhob_operation}
2626	* {axhoc_completion},
2627	{axhopt_typed_parameter},
2628	{axhopa_arg_parameter},
2629	{axhopm_motif_parameter},
2630	{axhoot_typed_output},
2631	{axhoom_motif_output},
2632	{axhoq_precondition},
2633	{axhog_guarantee},
2634	and
2635	{axhos_step}
2636	must appear within
2637	{axhob_operation}
2638	scope
2639	* No required ordering among completion, parameter, output, precondition, guarantee, and step markers
2640	* At most one
2641	{axhoc_completion}
2642	per operation
2643	
2644	=== Cross-Document Validation
2645	
2646	* {axhob_operation}
2647	attribute reference must match an operation defined with
2648	{axvo_procedure},
2649	{axvo_method},
2650	or
2651	{axvo_tabtarget}
2652	at the
2653	{axl_definition_site}
2654	* {axhob_operation}
2655	carries no dimensions — all dimensions are authoritative at the
2656	{axl_definition_site}
2657	only
2658	* When a
2659	{axvo_method}
2660	uses
2661	{axd_grouped},
2662	the method's affiliated entity must equal the group's affiliated entity
2663	
2664	== Axial Hierarchy Protocol Markers
2665	
2666	{axhp*} markers appear at the
2667	{axl_detail_site}
2668	of an
2669	{axi_protocol}
2670	as standalone annotations, paralleling the
2671	{axho*}
2672	operation hierarchy markers.
2673	A protocol's structural detail — the roster of transactions, and within each transaction the call envelope and return envelope — is expressed through a four-level nesting of these markers.
2674	
2675	=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2676	
2677	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2678	|===
2679	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

file.content:
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from the following text.

=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axhob_operation}          |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The operation this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
|{axhoc_completion}         |0                            |Introduces the signaling contract section (exit codes, status); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhopt_typed_parameter}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (zero new terms minted)
|{axhopa_arg_parameter}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses a shared CLI argument term (small vocabulary per spec)
|{axhopm_motif_parameter}   |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhoot_typed_output}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named output whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (parallel to typed_parameter pattern)
|{axhoom_motif_output}      |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhoq_precondition}       |0                            |A required condition before execution; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhog_guarantee}          |0                            |A promised condition after execution; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhos_step}               |0                            |A named behavioral step; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhos_waymark}            |0                            |A branch-targetable step with a local AsciiDoc anchor; bare marker, no lookahead
|===

[[axhob_operation]]
{axhob_operation}::
Opens the operation hierarchy block at the
{axl_detail_site}.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the operation this detail site describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axvo_procedure},
{axvo_method},
or
{axvo_tabtarget}
at the
{axl_definition_site}.
No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions are authoritative at the
{axl_definition_site}
only.

The surface syntax:

[source,asciidoc]
----
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_depot_levy}

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{rbtgo_depot_levy_name}
----

[[axhoc_completion]]
{axhoc_completion}::
Marks the signaling contract section in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; content follows directly.
Documents how the operation communicates success or failure: exit codes for CLI, HTTP status for REST, return values for functions.
Distinct from
{axhog_guarantee}
which describes state promises, and
{axhoot_typed_output}
/{axhoom_motif_output}
which describe produced data.
One per operation.

[[axhopt_typed_parameter]]
{axhopt_typed_parameter}::
Marks a named input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
No new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
Used when the parameter's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., a parameter that is simply a CIDR block or a domain name).
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhopa_arg_parameter]]
{axhopa_arg_parameter}::
Marks a named input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses a shared CLI argument term.
The attribute reference on the following line names a shared argument from the small CLI argument vocabulary defined per spec; no new term is minted.
Used when the parameter is a well-known CLI argument that appears across multiple operations.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing shared CLI argument anchor; small vocabulary per spec.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhopm_motif_parameter]]
{axhopm_motif_parameter}::
Marks an input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhoot_typed_output]]
{axhoot_typed_output}::
Marks a named output in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
Parallel to the
{axhopt_typed_parameter}
pattern: no new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
Used when the output's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., an output that is simply a list of names or a CIDR block).
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhoom_motif_output]]
{axhoom_motif_output}::
Marks an output in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
+
May carry
{axd_fact}
to indicate the output is delivered as a named fact file, or
{axd_tally}
to indicate a fact map of dynamically-named files sharing a common infix.

[[axhoq_precondition]]
{axhoq_precondition}::
Marks a required precondition in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Each precondition is a statement about required environmental state before execution.

[[axhog_guarantee]]
{axhog_guarantee}::
Marks a postcondition guarantee in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Each guarantee is a promise about environmental state after successful execution.

[[axhos_step]]
{axhos_step}::
Marks a named behavioral step in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Steps carry their identity through position and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
Branching between steps is expressed in prose using cross-references or by directing to a
{axhos_waymark}
anchor.

[[axhos_waymark]]
{axhos_waymark}::
Variant of
{axhos_step}
for steps that must be branch-targetable from other steps.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Rare; used when prose in another step says "go to step X" and needs a concrete anchor target.
Carries a local AsciiDoc anchor following the `operation_waymarkname` naming convention (e.g., `[[depot_levy_retry]]`).
The anchor enables intra-document cross-references without S0 elevation — the step remains at the detail site level.

=== Nesting Rules

Operation hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:

* {axhob_operation}
opens the operation block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
{axhob_operation}
* {axhoc_completion},
{axhopt_typed_parameter},
{axhopa_arg_parameter},
{axhopm_motif_parameter},
{axhoot_typed_output},
{axhoom_motif_output},
{axhoq_precondition},
{axhog_guarantee},
and
{axhos_step}
must appear within
{axhob_operation}
scope
* No required ordering among completion, parameter, output, precondition, guarantee, and step markers
* At most one
{axhoc_completion}
per operation

=== Cross-Document Validation

* {axhob_operation}
attribute reference must match an operation defined with
{axvo_procedure},
{axvo_method},
or
{axvo_tabtarget}
at the
{axl_definition_site}
* {axhob_operation}
carries no dimensions — all dimensions are authoritative at the
{axl_definition_site}
only
* When a
{axvo_method}
uses
{axd_grouped},
the method's affiliated entity must equal the group's affiliated entity

== Axial Hierarchy Protocol Markers

{axhp*} markers appear at the
{axl_detail_site}
of an
{axi_protocol}
as standalone annotations, paralleling the
{axho*}
operation hierarchy markers.
A protocol's structural detail — the roster of transactions, and within each transaction the call envelope and return envelope — is expressed through a four-level nesting of these markers.

=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

1330	
1331	Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
1332	privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
1333	file operations, interactive shells.
1334	
1335	Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
1336	station users. Lifecycle is short:
1337	{busn_conscript}
1338	when needed,
1339	{busn_discharge}
1340	when done.
1341	
1342	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1343	
1344	Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
1345	operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.
1346	
1347	==== Lifecycle
1348	
1349	[[bust_node_regime_list]]
1350	//axvo_tabtarget
1351	{bust_node_regime_list}::
1352	`buw-rnl` —
1353	List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.
1354	
1355	[[bust_node_regime_render]]
1356	//axvo_tabtarget
1357	{bust_node_regime_render}::
1358	`buw-rnr` —
1359	Render a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).
1360	
1361	[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
1362	//axvo_tabtarget
1363	{bust_node_regime_validate}::
1364	`buw-rnv` —
1365	Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).
1366	
1367	==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)
1368	
1369	[[bust_garrison_linux]]
1370	//axvo_tabtarget
1371	{bust_garrison_linux}::
1372	`buw-npgl` —
1373	{busn_garrison} ceremony for Linux nodes.
1374	
1375	[[bust_garrison_mac]]
1376	//axvo_tabtarget
1377	{bust_garrison_mac}::
1378	`buw-npgm` —
1379	{busn_garrison} ceremony for macOS nodes.
1380	
1381	[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
1382	//axvo_tabtarget
1383	{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
1384	`buw-npgc` —
1385	{busn_garrison} ceremony for Cygwin nodes.
1386	
1387	[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
1388	//axvo_tabtarget
1389	{bust_garrison_wsl}::
1390	`buw-npgw` —
1391	{busn_garrison} ceremony for WSL nodes.
1392	
1393	[[bust_garrison_powershell]]
1394	//axvo_tabtarget
1395	{bust_garrison_powershell}::
1396	`buw-npgp` —
1397	{busn_garrison} ceremony for PowerShell nodes.
1398	
1399	[[bust_garrison_localhost]]
1400	//axvo_tabtarget
1401	{bust_garrison_localhost}::
1402	`buw-npgx` —
1403	{busn_garrison} ceremony for localhost nodes.
1404	
1405	==== Workload (privileged authority required)
1406	
1407	[[bust_conscript_linux]]
1408	//axvo_tabtarget
1409	{bust_conscript_linux}::
1410	`buw-nwcl` —
1411	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Linux shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1412	
1413	[[bust_conscript_mac]]
1414	//axvo_tabtarget
1415	{bust_conscript_mac}::
1416	`buw-nwcm` —
1417	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a macOS shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1418	
1419	[[bust_conscript_cygwin]]
1420	//axvo_tabtarget
1421	{bust_conscript_cygwin}::
1422	`buw-nwcc` —
1423	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Cygwin shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1424	
1425	[[bust_conscript_wsl]]
1426	//axvo_tabtarget
1427	{bust_conscript_wsl}::
1428	`buw-nwcw` —
1429	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a WSL shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1430	
1431	[[bust_conscript_powershell]]
1432	//axvo_tabtarget
1433	{bust_conscript_powershell}::
1434	`buw-nwcp` —
1435	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a PowerShell shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1436	
1437	[[bust_conscript_localhost]]
1438	//axvo_tabtarget
1439	{bust_conscript_localhost}::
1440	`buw-nwcx` —
1441	{busn_conscript} a workload user with a localhost shell, via the named privileged BURN.
1442	
1443	[[bust_discharge]]
1444	//axvo_tabtarget
1445	{bust_discharge}::
1446	`buw-nwd` —
1447	{busn_discharge} a workload user.
1448	Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
1449	authority and removes the local profile.
1450	
1451	[[bust_inventory]]
1452	//axvo_tabtarget
1453	{bust_inventory}::
1454	`buw-nwi` —
1455	{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
1456	privileged BURN.
1457	
1458	==== Workload (operational)
1459	
1460	[[bust_knock]]
1461	//axvo_tabtarget
1462	{bust_knock}::
1463	`buw-nwk` —
1464	Probe workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.
1465	
1466	[[bust_remote_run]]
1467	//axvo_tabtarget
1468	{bust_remote_run}::
1469	`buw-nwr` —
1470	Run the contents of a command file on the remote node; capture output to
1471	{burd_output_dir}.
1472	
1473	[[bust_interactive_session]]
1474	//axvo_tabtarget
1475	{bust_interactive_session}::
1476	`buw-nws` —
1477	Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.
1478	
1479	==== Handbook
1480	
1481	[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
1482	//axvo_tabtarget
1483	{bust_handbook_node_top}::
1484	`buw-hn0` —
1485	Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.
1486	
1487	=== Privileges
1488	
1489	The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
1490	exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.
1491	
1492	A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
1493	alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
1494	is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.
1495	
1496	Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
1497	ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
1498	the Administrators group).
1499	
1500	=== Verb Definitions
1501	
1502	[[busn_garrison]]
1503	//axl_voices axt_command
1504	{busn_garrison}::
1505	Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
1506	{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
1507	admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
1508	node setup; idempotent on re-run.
1509	
1510	[[busn_conscript]]
1511	//axl_voices axt_command
1512	{busn_conscript}::
1513	Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
1514	{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
1515	the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
1516	where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
1517	privileged BURN's shell choice.
1518	
1519	[[busn_discharge]]
1520	//axl_voices axt_command
1521	{busn_discharge}::
1522	Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
1523	{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
1524	authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
1525	user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.
1526	
1527	[[busn_inventory]]
1528	//axl_voices axt_command
1529	{busn_inventory}::

file.content:
Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
file operations, interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)

[[bust_garrison_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_linux}::
`buw-npgl` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Linux nodes.

[[bust_garrison_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_mac}::
`buw-npgm` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for macOS nodes.

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-npgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Cygwin nodes.

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-npgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for WSL nodes.

[[bust_garrison_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_powershell}::
`buw-npgp` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for PowerShell nodes.

[[bust_garrison_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_localhost}::
`buw-npgx` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for localhost nodes.

==== Workload (privileged authority required)

[[bust_conscript_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_linux}::
`buw-nwcl` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Linux shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_mac}::
`buw-nwcm` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a macOS shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_cygwin}::
`buw-nwcc` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Cygwin shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_wsl}::
`buw-nwcw` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a WSL shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_powershell}::
`buw-nwcp` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a PowerShell shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_localhost}::
`buw-nwcx` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a localhost shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_discharge]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_discharge}::
`buw-nwd` —
{busn_discharge} a workload user.
Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
authority and removes the local profile.

[[bust_inventory]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_inventory}::
`buw-nwi` —
{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
privileged BURN.

==== Workload (operational)

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-nwk` —
Probe workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.

[[bust_remote_run]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_remote_run}::
`buw-nwr` —
Run the contents of a command file on the remote node; capture output to
{burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-nws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_node_top}::
`buw-hn0` —
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.

A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
node setup; idempotent on re-run.

[[busn_conscript]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_conscript}::
Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
privileged BURN's shell choice.

[[busn_discharge]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_discharge}::
Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.

[[busn_inventory]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_inventory}::

1	=== Windows Node Access
2	
3	Platform-specific ceremony and contracts for Windows node access.
4	
5	==== Status
6	
7	Stub.
8	Elaborate content to be drafted by subsequent paces — Windows is the
9	canonical worked example for the Remote Node Access ceremony, requiring
10	detailed coverage of OpenSSH server provisioning, the
11	`administrators_authorized_keys` special case, ACL fixes via `icacls`,
12	and the temp-password bootstrap handshake.
13	
14	==== Privilege Contract
15	
16	The {burn_user} of a Windows privileged BURN must be a member of the local
17	Administrators group.
18	Rationale: Windows OpenSSH consults `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
19	for users in the Administrators group, ignoring the per-user
20	`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` for those users.
21	A non-admin path would require parallel implementation with no return for
22	the supported use cases.
23	
24	Asserted at every ceremony participant before any modification.
25	
26	==== Platform Letters
27	
28	* `c` — Cygwin (`buw-npgc`, `buw-nwcc`)
29	* `w` — WSL (`buw-npgw`, `buw-nwcw`)
30	* `p` — PowerShell (`buw-npgp`, `buw-nwcp`)
31	
32	==== Out of Band
33	
34	The console phase of the Windows ceremony is performed at the Windows
35	physical keyboard (or RDP) by the operator and is described in the BUK
36	handbook surface, not in this spec.
37

file.content:
=== Windows Node Access

Platform-specific ceremony and contracts for Windows node access.

==== Status

Stub.
Elaborate content to be drafted by subsequent paces — Windows is the
canonical worked example for the Remote Node Access ceremony, requiring
detailed coverage of OpenSSH server provisioning, the
`administrators_authorized_keys` special case, ACL fixes via `icacls`,
and the temp-password bootstrap handshake.

==== Privilege Contract

The {burn_user} of a Windows privileged BURN must be a member of the local
Administrators group.
Rationale: Windows OpenSSH consults `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`
for users in the Administrators group, ignoring the per-user
`~/.ssh/authorized_keys` for those users.
A non-admin path would require parallel implementation with no return for
the supported use cases.

Asserted at every ceremony participant before any modification.

==== Platform Letters

* `c` — Cygwin (`buw-npgc`, `buw-nwcc`)
* `w` — WSL (`buw-npgw`, `buw-nwcw`)
* `p` — PowerShell (`buw-npgp`, `buw-nwcp`)

==== Out of Band

The console phase of the Windows ceremony is performed at the Windows
physical keyboard (or RDP) by the operator and is described in the BUK
handbook surface, not in this spec.

3380	
3381	This section specifies compositional requirements between motifs.
3382	When a term voices a particular motif, these rules define what companion elements are required.
3383	
3384	=== Operation Detail-Site Completeness
3385	
3386	A term voiced
3387	{axvo_procedure}
3388	or
3389	{axvo_method}
3390	with a detail-site subdocument requires:
3391	
3392	* {axhob_operation}
3393	— opens the hierarchy block, with lookahead to the operation's linked term
3394	
3395	* At least one
3396	{axhos_step}
3397	— what the operation does
3398	
3399	* {axhoc_completion}
3400	— how the operation signals success or failure
3401	
3402	Optional markers that MAY be included:
3403	
3404	* {axhopt_typed_parameter}
3405	/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
3406	/{axhopm_motif_parameter}
3407	— input parameters (typed/arg variants use 1-arity lookahead; motif is bare)
3408	
3409	* {axhoot_typed_output}
3410	/{axhoom_motif_output}
3411	— produced artifacts (typed variant uses 1-arity lookahead; motif is bare)
3412	
3413	* {axhoq_precondition}
3414	— environmental state requirements before execution
3415	
3416	* {axhog_guarantee}
3417	— environmental state guarantees after success
3418	
3419	=== CLI Subcommand Completeness
3420	
3421	Voicing as
3422	{axi_cli_subcommand}
3423	requires:
3424	
3425	* Exit Status section voiced as
3426	{axa_exit_uniform}
3427	or
3428	{axa_exit_enumerated}
3429	(which voice
3430	{axhoc_completion}
3431	in CLI context)
3432	
3433	* Arguments section voiced as
3434	{axa_argument_list}
3435	(which voices
3436	{axhopt_typed_parameter}
3437	/{axhopa_arg_parameter}
3438	in CLI context; may list zero arguments if operation takes none)
3439	
3440	=== CLI Option Completeness
3441	
3442	Voicing as
3443	{axa_cli_option}
3444	requires the definition text to specify:
3445	
3446	* Long form (e.g., `--file`)
3447	
3448	* Short form or explicit absence (e.g., `-f` or "none")
3449	
3450	* Default value, if the argument is used with
3451	{axd_defaulted}
3452	
3453	=== Argument List Item Qualification
3454	
3455	Within an
3456	{axa_argument_list},
3457	each item requires a preceding
3458	{axd_dimension}
3459	annotation specifying at minimum:
3460	
3461	* {axd_required}
3462	or
3463	{axd_optional}
3464	
3465	* {axd_defaulted}
3466	if a default value applies
3467	
3468	=== Type Voicing Qualification
3469	
3470	Voicing a type (subspecializing
3471	{axt_*}
3472	or
3473	{axtu_*}
3474	motifs) constrains dimension usage.
3475	
3476	Valid dimensions:
3477	
3478	* {axd_list}
3479	— type intrinsically represents delimited collection
3480	
3481	Invalid dimensions:
3482	
3483	* {axd_required},
3484	{axd_optional},
3485	{axd_defaulted}
3486	— presence applies to usage sites, not type definitions
3487	
3488	* {axd_repeated}
3489	— repetition applies to usage sites, not type definitions
3490	
3491	=== Regime Specification Completeness
3492	
3493	Voicing as
3494	{axrg_regime}
3495	requires:
3496	
3497	* Format dimension (
3498	{axf_bash},
3499	{axf_makefile},
3500	{axf_json},
3501	etc.) specifying how assignments are carried
3502	
3503	* Provenance dimension (
3504	{axrd_file_sourced},
3505	{axrd_env_inherited},
3506	or
3507	{axrd_constructed})
3508	declaring how
3509	{axrg_assignment_s}
3510	arrive
3511	
3512	=== Regime Variable Qualification
3513	
3514	Voicing as
3515	{axrg_variable}
3516	requires:
3517	
3518	* Type in annotation (base type motif or type voicing)
3519	
3520	* At minimum
3521	{axd_required}
3522	or
3523	{axd_optional}
3524	
3525	* {axd_defaulted}
3526	if a default value applies
3527	
3528	Valid dimensions:
3529	
3530	* {axd_required}
3531	— variable must have assignment
3532	
3533	* {axd_optional}
3534	— variable may be absent
3535	
3536	* {axd_defaulted}
3537	— fallback value when absent
3538	
3539	* {axd_list}
3540	— inherited from type voicing; variable's type is a delimited collection
3541	
3542	Invalid dimensions:
3543	
3544	* {axd_repeated}
3545	— regime files use unique keys; multiple assignments of same variable not permitted
3546	
3547	* {axd_nullable}
3548	— format-dependent;
3549	{axf_bash}
3550	has no null distinct from empty string
3551	
3552	=== Regime Voicing Annotation Completeness
3553	
3554	{axvr_regime}
3555	requires:
3556	
3557	* {axf_format}
3558	dimension specifying how
3559	{axrg_assignment_s}
3560	are carried
3561	
3562	{axvr_variable}
3563	requires:
3564	
3565	* Second
3566	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3567	in definition text must be the parent regime
3568	
3569	* {axd_required}
3570	or
3571	{axd_optional}
3572	
3573	* Type
3574	{axl_motif}
3575	(base type or type voicing)
3576	
3577	{axvr_group}
3578	requires:
3579	
3580	* Second
3581	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3582	in definition text must be the parent regime
3583	
3584	=== Regime Hierarchy Consistency
3585	
3586	* {axvr_group}
3587	with
3588	{axd_conditional}
3589	requires corresponding
3590	{axhrgb_group}
3591	in subdocument to have at least one
3592	{axhrgc_gate}
3593	
3594	* {axvr_group}
3595	without
3596	{axd_conditional}:
3597	corresponding
3598	{axhrgb_group}
3599	must have no
3600	{axhrgc_gate}
3601	
3602	* Every
3603	{axvr_variable}
3604	under a regime should appear as either
3605	{axhrv_variable}
3606	or
3607	{axhrgv_variable}
3608	in the subdocument
3609	
3610	=== Operation Voicing Annotation Completeness
3611	
3612	{axvo_procedure}
3613	requires:
3614	
3615	* Exactly one lifecycle dimension:
3616	{axd_transient},
3617	{axd_longrunning},
3618	or
3619	{axd_periodic}
3620	
3621	* If
3622	{axd_grouped}:
3623	second
3624	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3625	in definition text must be an
3626	{axo_group}
3627	
3628	{axvo_method}
3629	requires:
3630	
3631	* Second
3632	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3633	in definition text must be the affiliated
3634	{axo_entity}
3635	
3636	* Exactly one lifecycle dimension:
3637	{axd_transient},
3638	{axd_longrunning},
3639	or
3640	{axd_periodic}
3641	
3642	* If
3643	{axd_grouped}:
3644	third
3645	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3646	in definition text must be an
3647	{axo_group}
3648	
3649	{axvo_group}
3650	requires:
3651	
3652	* Second
3653	{mcm_attribute_reference}
3654	in definition text must be the affiliated
3655	{axo_entity}
3656	
3657	* Optional exposure dimension:
3658	{axd_tabtarget},
3659	{axd_slash_command},
3660	or other interface mechanism specifying the default exposure for external members
3661	
3662	=== Operation Hierarchy Consistency
3663	
3664	* {axhob_operation}
3665	attribute reference must match a term voiced
3666	{axvo_procedure},
3667	{axvo_method},
3668	or
3669	{axvo_tabtarget}
3670	at the
3671	{axl_definition_site}
3672	
3673	* Every
3674	{axhoc_completion},
3675	{axhopt_typed_parameter},
3676	{axhopa_arg_parameter},
3677	{axhopm_motif_parameter},
3678	{axhoot_typed_output},
3679	{axhoom_motif_output},
3680	{axhoq_precondition},
3681	{axhog_guarantee},
3682	and
3683	{axhos_step}
3684	must appear within
3685	{axhob_operation}
3686	scope
3687

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{scr_dns_step}
3	
4	Configures sentry DNS resolution via `/etc/resolv.conf` pointing to {rbrr_dns_server}.
5	
6	If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is disabled, the {at_sentry_container}:
7	
8	* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1 (UDP and TCP port 53)
9	* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to eth0 (UDP and TCP port 53)
10	
11	If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global or allowlist, the {at_sentry_container} executes this sequence:
12	
13	//axhos_step
14	. Dnsmasq Configuration:
15	+
16	--
17	* Core Settings:
18	** Bind strictly to {at_enclave_network} interface eth1
19	** Listen on {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
20	** DNS service only — all DHCP functionality disabled (no-dhcp-interface)
21	** Isolated from host resolver (no-resolv)
22	** Exclude eth0 from listening (except-interface)
23	** Security: bogus-priv, domain-needed, strict-order
24	** Forwarding concurrency: dns-forward-max=150
25	** Source port range: 4096-65535
26	
27	* Cache Settings:
28	** Cache size: 1000 entries
29	** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
30	** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds
31	
32	* Logging (all modes):
33	** log-queries=extra
34	** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
35	** log-dhcp, log-debug, log-async=20
36	
37	* Query Control:
38	** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global:
39	*** Forward all queries to {rbrr_dns_server}
40	*** Return all responses unmodified
41	
42	** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is allowlist:
43	*** Resolve-then-freeze: for each domain in {rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}, resolve A records via {rbrr_dns_server} at startup, then freeze as static `address=` entries in dnsmasq. No `server=` forwarding directives — dnsmasq synthesizes responses locally from frozen IPs. Subdomain queries (e.g., `exfil.anthropic.com`) return the frozen parent IP without generating upstream queries, closing the DNS subdomain exfiltration channel.
44	*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries (address=/#/)
45	--
46	
47	//axhos_step
48	. Service Launch:
49	* Start dnsmasq daemon
50	* Wait for initialization (2 second delay)
51	
52	//axhos_step
53	. Filter Rules:
54	+
55	--
56	* Accept DNS queries arriving at sentry from enclave:
57	** RBM-INGRESS: eth1 UDP port 53
58	** RBM-INGRESS: eth1 TCP port 53
59	* Accept sentry upstream DNS forwarding:
60	** RBM-EGRESS: eth0 UDP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
61	** RBM-EGRESS: eth0 TCP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
62	--
63

file.content:
//axhob_operation
{scr_dns_step}

Configures sentry DNS resolution via `/etc/resolv.conf` pointing to {rbrr_dns_server}.

If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is disabled, the {at_sentry_container}:

* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-FORWARD from eth1 (UDP and TCP port 53)
* Blocks all DNS traffic in RBM-EGRESS to eth0 (UDP and TCP port 53)

If {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global or allowlist, the {at_sentry_container} executes this sequence:

//axhos_step
. Dnsmasq Configuration:
+
--
* Core Settings:
** Bind strictly to {at_enclave_network} interface eth1
** Listen on {rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}
** DNS service only — all DHCP functionality disabled (no-dhcp-interface)
** Isolated from host resolver (no-resolv)
** Exclude eth0 from listening (except-interface)
** Security: bogus-priv, domain-needed, strict-order
** Forwarding concurrency: dns-forward-max=150
** Source port range: 4096-65535

* Cache Settings:
** Cache size: 1000 entries
** Minimum TTL: 600 seconds
** Maximum TTL: 3600 seconds

* Logging (all modes):
** log-queries=extra
** log-facility=/var/log/dnsmasq.log
** log-dhcp, log-debug, log-async=20

* Query Control:
** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is global:
*** Forward all queries to {rbrr_dns_server}
*** Return all responses unmodified

** When {rbrn_uplink_dns_mode} is allowlist:
*** Resolve-then-freeze: for each domain in {rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}, resolve A records via {rbrr_dns_server} at startup, then freeze as static `address=` entries in dnsmasq. No `server=` forwarding directives — dnsmasq synthesizes responses locally from frozen IPs. Subdomain queries (e.g., `exfil.anthropic.com`) return the frozen parent IP without generating upstream queries, closing the DNS subdomain exfiltration channel.
*** Return NXDOMAIN for all other queries (address=/#/)
--

//axhos_step
. Service Launch:
* Start dnsmasq daemon
* Wait for initialization (2 second delay)

//axhos_step
. Filter Rules:
+
--
* Accept DNS queries arriving at sentry from enclave:
** RBM-INGRESS: eth1 UDP port 53
** RBM-INGRESS: eth1 TCP port 53
* Accept sentry upstream DNS forwarding:
** RBM-EGRESS: eth0 UDP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
** RBM-EGRESS: eth0 TCP port 53 to {rbrr_dns_server}
--

aiTitle: Find AXLA template with hierarchy markers

content.text:
Found. Template lives in **AXLA-Lexicon.adoc §Axial Hierarchy Operation Markers** (lines 2463-2663). The docket's "procedure/method hierarchy markers" = the `axho*` family.

**The detail-site shape** (procedure/method/tabtarget all share it):

```
//axhob_operation
{operation_quoin}

[free prose / NOTE block describing the operation]

//axhos_step
. Step name
   ... step body ...

//axhos_step
. Next step
   ...

//axhoc_completion
[success/failure signaling contract]
```

**Required minimum** for any operation subdoc (AXLA §Operation Detail-Site Completeness, lines 3384-3417):
- `axhob_operation` opens the block (1-arity lookahead = the tabtarget quoin)
- ≥1 `axhos_step`
- `axhoc_completion`

**Optional markers** at the detail site:
- `axhopt_typed_parameter` / `axhopa_arg_parameter` / `axhopm_motif_parameter` — input parameters
- `axhoot_typed_output` / `axhoom_motif_output` — produced artifacts
- `axhoq_precondition` — pre-state requirements
- `axhog_guarantee` — post-state promises
- `axhos_waymark` — branch-targetable step variant

**Existing parallel subdocs to crib from** — 50+ files use this shape today. Two clean examples:
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDR-director_roster.adoc` (steps + completion + opening NOTE; closest to a "command runs steps, signals exit" tabtarget shape)
- `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSDS-dns_step.adoc` (more procedural, conditional steps, no completion — older shape, less suitable as template)

RBSDR is the better template — it has the full `axhob → NOTE → axhos_step×N → axhoc_completion` arc.

**One spec point worth flagging**: the docket's `axhob_operation` lookahead requires the referenced anchor to be voiced `axvo_procedure | axvo_method | axvo_tabtarget` at the definition site (AXLA line 2489-2495). The 15 BUS0 voicings are all `axvo_tabtarget` — so `axhob_operation` cleanly anchors them. No new voicings needed.

**Now ready to** (your call):
1. Confirm the **15 quoins** by running the `grep '^:bust_' ...` discovery I'd planned, and
2. Mint the **two-letter `xx` mnemonics** for `BUSTxx-<silks>.adoc` filenames

Want me to proceed with discovery + mnemonic table, or pause here?

message.content: proceed.

aiTitle: Find AXLA template with hierarchy markers

content.text:
Discovery confirmed: 22 `bust_*` voicings in BUS0; subtracting the abstract parent `bust_tabtarget` (line 36, before the `// Concrete BURN Tabtargets` divider) and 6 platform-deferred (`_linux`, `_mac`, `_localhost` × garrison/conscript) yields exactly **15 in scope** — matches docket.

**Proposed mnemonic legend** (X = category, Y = variant, Y-monosemy held across the BUST domain):

```
X (category)              Y (variant)
L = Lifecycle             L = list           E = execute (run)
G = Garrison              R = render         S = ssh/session
C = Conscript             V = validate       T = top
W = Workload generic      C = cygwin
O = Operational           W = wsl
H = Handbook              P = powershell
                          D = discharge
                          I = inventory
                          K = knock
```

Y monosemy verified: every Y letter has exactly one meaning across all 15 children. (X/Y position overlap — `C` means Conscript at X, Cygwin at Y — is per-position-disjoint, which the spec permits.)

**The 15 mnemonic table** (filename uses PascalCase silks, matching the existing BUSN convention `BUSNW-NodeWindows`):

| Mnemonic | Quoin (BUS0) | Filename |
|----------|--------------|----------|
| LL | bust_node_regime_list | BUSTLL-NodeRegimeList.adoc |
| LR | bust_node_regime_render | BUSTLR-NodeRegimeRender.adoc |
| LV | bust_node_regime_validate | BUSTLV-NodeRegimeValidate.adoc |
| GC | bust_garrison_cygwin | BUSTGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc |
| GW | bust_garrison_wsl | BUSTGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc |
| GP | bust_garrison_powershell | BUSTGP-GarrisonPowershell.adoc |
| CC | bust_conscript_cygwin | BUSTCC-ConscriptCygwin.adoc |
| CW | bust_conscript_wsl | BUSTCW-ConscriptWsl.adoc |
| CP | bust_conscript_powershell | BUSTCP-ConscriptPowershell.adoc |
| WD | bust_discharge | BUSTWD-Discharge.adoc |
| WI | bust_inventory | BUSTWI-Inventory.adoc |
| OK | bust_knock | BUSTOK-Knock.adoc |
| OE | bust_remote_run | BUSTOE-RemoteRun.adoc |
| OS | bust_interactive_session | BUSTOS-InteractiveSession.adoc |
| HT | bust_handbook_node_top | BUSTHT-HandbookNodeTop.adoc |

**Three things worth flagging before I generate**:

1. **`bust_discharge`/`bust_inventory` X-letter**: I gave them `W` (Workload generic, no platform variant). The closest BUS0 catalog header is "Workload (privileged authority required)" — so W reads naturally. Alternative: bucket them under a single category letter with the operational ones. Current split keeps the BUS0 catalog grouping visible.

2. **`OE` for remote_run vs reusing `R`**: `R` is taken by Lifecycle-render under Y monosemy. `OE` (Execute) avoids the collision and is faithful to the operation ("run a command file"). Could also be `OR` if we accept Y-collision — recommend not.

3. **PascalCase silks vs snake_case**: I chose PascalCase to match BUSNW/BUSNL/BUSNM. RBS family uses snake (RBSDR-director_roster). Both are present in the repo. PascalCase keeps BUS-family local consistency.

Sign off on the mnemonic legend + table, or push back on any of those three? Once approved I'll generate the 15 subdocs from the RBSDR-style template, add the `include::` lines to BUS0, and run `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh`.

file.content:
==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_node_top}::
`buw-hn0` —
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.

A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
node setup; idempotent on re-run.

[[busn_conscript]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_conscript}::
Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
privileged BURN's shell choice.

[[busn_discharge]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_discharge}::
Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.

[[busn_inventory]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_inventory}::
List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.

include::BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc[]

include::BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc[]

1305	
1306	== Remote Node Access
1307	
1308	Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
1309	trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
1310	workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.
1311	
1312	=== Privilege Tiers
1313	
1314	The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
1315	as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
1316	tier at entry.
1317	
1318	==== Privileged Tier
1319	
1320	Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
1321	privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
1322	provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.
1323	
1324	Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
1325	format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
1326	station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
1327	enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.
1328	
1329	==== Workload Tier
1330	
1331	Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
1332	privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
1333	file operations, interactive shells.
1334	
1335	Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
1336	station users. Lifecycle is short:
1337	{busn_conscript}
1338	when needed,
1339	{busn_discharge}
1340	when done.
1341	
1342	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1343	
1344	Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
1345	operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.
1346	
1347	==== Lifecycle
1348	
1349	[[bust_node_regime_list]]
1350	//axvo_tabtarget
1351	{bust_node_regime_list}::
1352	`buw-rnl` —
1353	List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.
1354	
1355	[[bust_node_regime_render]]
1356	//axvo_tabtarget
1357	{bust_node_regime_render}::
1358	`buw-rnr` —
1359	Render a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

file.content:
{burx_exit_status}::
Unix exit code of the dispatched command.
Absence indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_ended_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_ended_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
Same format as
{burx_began_at}.
Present only when
{burx_exit_status}
is present.

include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]

== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
tier at entry.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
file operations, interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).
