aiTitle: Find AXLA template with hierarchy markers

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_abjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation formally renounces an
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	from
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by enumerating and deleting every package beneath the ark's
9	hallmark subtree via the GAR REST API implemented by
10	{gar_service}.
11	
12	An
13	{rbtga_ark}
14	is the coherent unit of a Recipe Bottle build: a set of packages rooted at
15	`hallmarks/«HALLMARK»/` and distinguished by basename.
16	The
17	{rbtga_ark_image}
18	(basename `image`) contains the container image itself.
19	The
20	{rbtga_ark_about}
21	(basename `about`) contains SBOM and build metadata.
22	The
23	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
24	(basename `vouch`) contains verification results.
25	The
26	{rbtga_ark_pouch}
27	(basename `pouch`) contains the build context (conjure/bind only, absent for graft).
28	A fifth basename, `diags`, is present for conjure arks only (build-time diagnostics forwarded to the about pipeline).
29	For conjure vessels, a single `attest` package holds per-platform tags (`attest:«HALLMARK»-«ARCH»`).
30	Abjuring an ark deletes every package discovered beneath the hallmark subtree as a single logical operation.
31	
32	The
33	{rbtr_director}
34	abjures an ark:
35	
36	//axhopt_typed_parameter
37	{rbtga_ark}
38	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
39	.. {rbbc_require}
40	`«INPUT_VESSEL»` provided as first argument (retained for AAK signature compatibility; not used to construct the subtree path, drops in AAL)
41	.. {rbbc_require}
42	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` provided as second argument
43	.. {rbbc_fatal}
44	if hallmark missing: "Hallmark parameter required"
45	
46	//axhopt_typed_parameter
47	{rbtga_hallmark}
48	. *Validate Hallmark Format*:
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	`«INPUT_HALLMARK»` matches pattern `[cbg]\d{12}-r\d{12}` (mode prefix + dual timestamp)
51	.. {rbbc_fatal}
52	if malformed: "Hallmark format invalid"
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Authenticate as
56	{rbtr_director}*:
57	.. Execute
58	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
59	.. {rbbc_store}
60	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Construct Hallmark Subtree*:
64	.. {rbbc_store}
65	`«SUBTREE»` as `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«INPUT_HALLMARK»/` where `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»` derives from `RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT` (kindled from `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` + `hallmarks`)
66	.. {rbbc_store}
67	`«REPO_BASE»` as the GAR REST API repository base (`projects/«PROJECT»/locations/«REGION»/repositories/«REPOSITORY»`)
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Enumerate Packages Under Subtree*:
71	.. {rbbc_call}
72	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list] with:
73	... method: `GET`
74	... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages?pageSize=1000`
75	... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
76	.. {rbbc_require}
77	HTTP 200 response
78	.. Extract `.packages[].name` from the response, strip the resource prefix, and URL-decode `%2F` back to `/`
79	.. {rbbc_store}
80	`«ARK_PACKAGES»` as the list of decoded package names whose prefix equals `«SUBTREE»`
81	.. {rbbc_fatal}
82	if `«ARK_PACKAGES»` is empty: "No packages found under «SUBTREE» — hallmark not present in registry"
83	
84	//axhos_step
85	. *Confirm Abjuration*:
86	.. Unless `--force` flag provided:
87	... {rbbc_show}
88	"Will abjure «COUNT» packages under «SUBTREE»:" followed by each entry in `«ARK_PACKAGES»`
89	... {rbbc_require}
90	user types `yes` to confirm
91	... {rbbc_fatal}
92	if not confirmed: "prompt not confirmed."
93	
94	//axhos_step
95	. *Delete Each Enumerated Package*:
96	.. For each `«PACKAGE»` in `«ARK_PACKAGES»`:
97	... {rbbc_store}
98	`«ENCODED»` as `«PACKAGE»` with `/` replaced by `%2F`
99	... {rbbc_call}
100	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/delete[GAR REST API: packages.delete] with:
101	.... method: `DELETE`
102	.... endpoint: `«REPO_BASE»/packages/«ENCODED»`
103	.... header: `Authorization: Bearer «DIRECTOR_TOKEN»`
104	... {rbbc_require}
105	HTTP 200 response (deletion returns a long-running operation; the 200 is treated as accepted)
106	... {rbbc_fatal}
107	if delete fails: "Failed to delete package «PACKAGE» (HTTP «STATUS_CODE»)"
108	
109	//axhoot_typed_output
110	{rbtga_ark}
111	. *Display Results*:
112	.. {rbbc_show}
113	"Hallmark abjured: «INPUT_HALLMARK» («COUNT» packages)"
114	.. For each deleted package:
115	... {rbbc_show}
116	`  - «PACKAGE» deleted`
117	
118	//axhoc_completion
119	Procedure completes successfully when every package discovered beneath the hallmark subtree has been deleted from the registry and the abjuration confirmation is displayed.
120	The ark is now formally renounced and no longer part of the repository corpus.
121	Because enumeration drives the delete set, there is no separate existence check and no orphan-detection branch: whatever lives in the subtree is removed, and an empty subtree is itself the "not present" signal.
122	Deleting a package removes all of its tags in one call — per-basename HEAD/DELETE dances are no longer required.
123

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_access_jwt_probe}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation probes
5	{gar_service}
6	read access for a specified
7	{rbtr_role}
8	by performing a JWT-to-OAuth token exchange and then calling the Artifact Registry packages.list API.
9	It may repeat for a configured number of iterations with a configurable delay, enabling sustained access verification and token lifecycle testing.
10	
11	The operation accepts a role parameter selecting among
12	{rbtr_governor},
13	{rbtr_director},
14	and
15	{rbtr_retriever},
16	each with Owner, repoAdmin, and reader permissions respectively.
17	All three roles have Artifact Registry read access, so a successful response confirms both token validity and role-specific permission.
18	
19	//axhos_step
20	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
21	.. {rbbc_require}
22	`«INPUT_ROLE»` provided as first argument: one of `governor`, `director`, or `retriever`
23	.. {rbbc_fatal}
24	if role missing: "Role parameter required (governor|director|retriever)"
25	.. {rbbc_fatal}
26	if role unrecognized: "Unknown role: «INPUT_ROLE» — must be governor, director, or retriever"
27	.. {rbbc_store}
28	`«ITERATION_COUNT»` from `--count` flag (default: 1)
29	.. {rbbc_store}
30	`«DELAY_MS»` from `--delay_ms` flag (default: 0)
31	.. {rbbc_fatal}
32	if `«ITERATION_COUNT»` is not a positive integer: "Count must be a positive integer"
33	.. {rbbc_fatal}
34	if `«DELAY_MS»` is not a non-negative integer: "Delay must be a non-negative integer"
35	
36	//axhos_step
37	. *Resolve Role Configuration*:
38	.. If `«INPUT_ROLE»` is `governor`:
39	... {rbbc_store}
40	`«ROLE_DISPLAY»` as `Governor`
41	... {rbbc_store}
42	`«RBRA_FILE»` from
43	{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}
44	.. If `«INPUT_ROLE»` is `director`:
45	... {rbbc_store}
46	`«ROLE_DISPLAY»` as `Director`
47	... {rbbc_store}
48	`«RBRA_FILE»` from
49	{rbrr_director_rbra_file}
50	.. If `«INPUT_ROLE»` is `retriever`:
51	... {rbbc_store}
52	`«ROLE_DISPLAY»` as `Retriever`
53	... {rbbc_store}
54	`«RBRA_FILE»` from
55	{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}
56	.. {rbbc_require}
57	`«RBRA_FILE»` exists on the filesystem
58	.. {rbbc_fatal}
59	if file missing: "«ROLE_DISPLAY» RBRA file missing at «RBRA_FILE»"
60	
61	//axhos_step
62	. *Construct Verification Endpoint*:
63	.. {rbbc_store}
64	`«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}` (resolved by `RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`)
65	.. {rbbc_store}
66	`«GAR_REPOSITORY»` as `${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}-gar` (resolved by `RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY`)
67	.. {rbbc_store}
68	`«VERIFY_URL»` as:
69	+
70	`https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com/v1/projects/«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID»/locations/{rbrr_gcp_region}/repositories/«GAR_REPOSITORY»/packages`
71	
72	//axhos_step
73	. *Execute Probe Iterations*:
74	.. For each iteration `«I»` from 1 to `«ITERATION_COUNT»`:
75	
76	... *Acquire «ROLE_DISPLAY» Token*:
77	.... Execute
78	{rbtoe_jwt_oauth_exchange}
79	with `«RBRA_FILE»`
80	.... {rbbc_store}
81	`«ROLE_TOKEN»` from exchange result
82	.... {rbbc_fatal}
83	if token exchange fails: "Iteration «I»: JWT-to-OAuth exchange failed for «ROLE_DISPLAY»"
84	
85	... *Call Artifact Registry Packages API*:
86	.... {rbbc_call}
87	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[artifactregistry.projects.locations.repositories.packages.list] with:
88	..... method: `GET`
89	..... endpoint: `«VERIFY_URL»`
90	..... header: `Authorization: Bearer «ROLE_TOKEN»`
91	.... {rbbc_store}
92	`«HTTP_STATUS»` from response status code
93	.... {rbbc_store}
94	`«RESPONSE_BODY»` from response body
95	
96	... *Evaluate Response*:
97	.... If `«HTTP_STATUS»` is 200:
98	..... {rbbc_show}
99	`Iteration «I»/«ITERATION_COUNT»: «ROLE_DISPLAY» OK — packages.list HTTP 200`
100	.... If `«HTTP_STATUS»` is 403:
101	..... {rbbc_fatal}
102	"Iteration «I»: «ROLE_DISPLAY» access denied — HTTP 403 (token valid but role lacks Artifact Registry read permission)"
103	.... If `«HTTP_STATUS»` is 401:
104	..... {rbbc_fatal}
105	"Iteration «I»: «ROLE_DISPLAY» token rejected — HTTP 401 (token exchange succeeded but token is invalid)"
106	.... If `«HTTP_STATUS»` is one of 500, 502, 503, or 504 (transient server-side error):
107	..... Re-execute the {rbbc_call} up to 3 total attempts, sleeping with exponential backoff between attempts (2s, then 4s)
108	..... On any non-5xx response within the retry window, re-enter *Evaluate Response* with that status
109	..... {rbbc_fatal}
110	if all 3 attempts return a 5xx status: "Iteration «I»: «ROLE_DISPLAY» repeated transient HTTP «HTTP_STATUS» after 3 attempts"
111	.... Otherwise:
112	..... {rbbc_fatal}
113	"Iteration «I»: «ROLE_DISPLAY» unexpected HTTP «HTTP_STATUS»: «RESPONSE_BODY»"
114	
115	... *Apply Inter-Iteration Delay* (if not final iteration):
116	.... If `«DELAY_MS»` is greater than 0 and `«I»` is less than `«ITERATION_COUNT»`:
117	..... {rbbc_show}
118	`Sleeping «DELAY_MS»ms before next iteration`
119	..... Sleep `«DELAY_MS»` milliseconds
120	
121	//axhos_step
122	. *Display Summary*:
123	.. {rbbc_show}
124	`«ROLE_DISPLAY» JWT access probe complete: «ITERATION_COUNT»/«ITERATION_COUNT» iterations passed`
125	
126	//axhoc_completion
127	Procedure completes successfully when all iterations confirm that the selected role's JWT-derived OAuth token grants read access to the
128	{gar_service}
129	packages list endpoint.
130	Completion demonstrates both that JWT-to-OAuth token exchange is operational for the role and that the resulting token carries the expected Artifact Registry read permissions.
131

content.content:
700	//axl_voices axt_enum_value
701	{buf_ext_alias}::
702	Registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURN alias observation facts (`BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"`).
703	Written by `burn_list_capture` (in `burn_regime.sh`) once per discovered alias profile, with empty content — presence-of-file is the fact, not file content.
704	+
705	First and currently sole member of the
706	{busff_multi_fact}
707	registry; serves as the proof-of-shape for the convention.
708	
709	=== Yelp Pattern
710	
711	[[busy_yelp]]
712	//axl_voices axk_premise
713	{busy_yelp}::
714	A formatted string fragment for interpolation into `buh_line` calls.
715	Yelp content **must not contain literal `%` characters** — this premise allows safe interpolation into `printf` format strings via bash variable expansion.
716	+
717	Produced by
718	{busy_yawp_s}
719	via pure assignment of inert
720	{busy_diastema}
721	markers, resolved at display time by
722	{busy_format_yawp}.
723	+
724	Yelps are the vocabulary-first primitive for handbook output.
725	A kit defines vocabulary constants at kindle time by capturing yelps into readonly globals (e.g., `RBYC_DEPOT`, `RBYC_HALLMARK`).
726	Call sites compose lines by interpolating these constants into `buh_line` strings.
727	
728	[[busy_yawp]]
729	//axl_voices axo_entity
730	{busy_yawp}::
731	A `buyy_*_yawp` function in `buym_yelp.sh` that produces a
732	{busy_yelp}
733	via pure bash assignment into the group return variable `z_buym_yelp`.
734	No subshells, no stdout, no process spawning — cannot fail.
735	+
736	Yawp functions stamp inert
737	{busy_diastema}
738	markers into strings.
739	All terminal capability decisions are deferred to
740	{busy_format_yawp}
741	at display time.
742	Eight yawp functions exist: `buyy_cmd_yawp` (command), `buyy_ui_yawp` (UI element), `buyy_href_yawp` (web hyperlink), `buyy_link_yawp` (vocabulary link), `buyy_tt_yawp` (resolved tabtarget), `buyy_pass_yawp` (pass/green), `buyy_warn_yawp` (warn/orange), `buyy_fail_yawp` (fail/red).
743	
744	[[busy_diastema]]
745	//axl_voices axo_entity
746	{busy_diastema}::
747	A non-printing byte sequence (`\x02\x1N`) embedded in a
748	{busy_yelp}
749	by a
750	{busy_yawp}
751	to delimit styled regions.
752	Eight markers form the diastema family: CMD, UI, HREF_URL, HREF_TEXT, LINK_URL, LINK_TEXT, TT, and END.
753	Openers identify the region type; END returns to the ambient color.
754	HREF and LINK have separate markers because vocabulary links (teaching terms) and web links (external references) serve different pedagogical purposes and carry independent palette slots.
755	
756	[[busy_format_yawp]]
757	//axl_voices axo_entity
758	{busy_format_yawp}::
759	The single intelligence point for yelp display: `buyf_format_yawp(color, string)`.

//axl_voices axt_enum_value
{buf_ext_alias}::
Registered BUK-domain extension constant for BURN alias observation facts (`BUF_EXT_ALIAS="buf_ext_alias"`).
Written by `burn_list_capture` (in `burn_regime.sh`) once per discovered alias profile, with empty content — presence-of-file is the fact, not file content.
+
First and currently sole member of the
{busff_multi_fact}
registry; serves as the proof-of-shape for the convention.

=== Yelp Pattern

[[busy_yelp]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{busy_yelp}::
A formatted string fragment for interpolation into `buh_line` calls.
Yelp content **must not contain literal `%` characters** — this premise allows safe interpolation into `printf` format strings via bash variable expansion.
+
Produced by
{busy_yawp_s}
via pure assignment of inert
{busy_diastema}
markers, resolved at display time by
{busy_format_yawp}.
+
Yelps are the vocabulary-first primitive for handbook output.
A kit defines vocabulary constants at kindle time by capturing yelps into readonly globals (e.g., `RBYC_DEPOT`, `RBYC_HALLMARK`).
Call sites compose lines by interpolating these constants into `buh_line` strings.

[[busy_yawp]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busy_yawp}::
A `buyy_*_yawp` function in `buym_yelp.sh` that produces a
{busy_yelp}
via pure bash assignment into the group return variable `z_buym_yelp`.
No subshells, no stdout, no process spawning — cannot fail.
+
Yawp functions stamp inert
{busy_diastema}
markers into strings.
All terminal capability decisions are deferred to
{busy_format_yawp}
at display time.
Eight yawp functions exist: `buyy_cmd_yawp` (command), `buyy_ui_yawp` (UI element), `buyy_href_yawp` (web hyperlink), `buyy_link_yawp` (vocabulary link), `buyy_tt_yawp` (resolved tabtarget), `buyy_pass_yawp` (pass/green), `buyy_warn_yawp` (warn/orange), `buyy_fail_yawp` (fail/red).

[[busy_diastema]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busy_diastema}::
A non-printing byte sequence (`\x02\x1N`) embedded in a
{busy_yelp}
by a
{busy_yawp}
to delimit styled regions.
Eight markers form the diastema family: CMD, UI, HREF_URL, HREF_TEXT, LINK_URL, LINK_TEXT, TT, and END.
Openers identify the region type; END returns to the ambient color.
HREF and LINK have separate markers because vocabulary links (teaching terms) and web links (external references) serve different pedagogical purposes and carry independent palette slots.

[[busy_format_yawp]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busy_format_yawp}::
The single intelligence point for yelp display: `buyf_format_yawp(color, string)`.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_director_vouch}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation vouches for every *pending*
5	{rbtga_ark}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository},
8	where *pending* means a hallmark whose subtree contains both
9	{rbtga_ark_image}
10	and
11	{rbtga_ark_about}
12	basenames but no
13	{rbtga_ark_vouch}.
14	The
15	{rbtr_director}
16	enumerates the full hallmark corpus via a single GAR REST `packages.list` call (the same shared helper used by
17	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}),
18	identifies pending hallmarks, resolves the vessel name from each hallmark's
19	{rbtga_ark_about}
20	metadata (since vessel identity no longer lives in the GAR path), and dispatches
21	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
22	per hallmark.
23	Mode-awareness (conjure, bind, graft) lives inside
24	{rbtgo_ark_vouch};
25	batch vouch does not branch on mode.
26	
27	NOTE: *Fail-fast semantics.* If
28	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
29	fails for any hallmark, the batch terminates via fatal error at that hallmark.
30	There is no skip-with-warning path and no summary-count-masking of failures: a broken hallmark demands immediate attention, not quiet continuation.
31	
32	The
33	{rbtr_director}
34	batch-vouches arks:
35	
36	//axhos_step
37	. *Authenticate as
38	{rbtr_director}*:
39	.. Execute
40	{rbtoe_director_authenticate}
41	.. {rbbc_store}
42	`«DIRECTOR_TOKEN»` for subsequent API calls
43	
44	//axhos_step
45	. *Enumerate Hallmark Packages*:
46	.. {rbbc_call}
47	the shared hallmark enumeration helper — a single
48	link:https://cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.repositories.packages/list[GAR REST API: packages.list]
49	call under `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/` (`RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT`), filtered to `<hallmark>/<basename>` shape
50	.. {rbbc_store}
51	`«PAIRS»` as sorted `<hallmark> <basename>` pairs (same shape produced for
52	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally})
53	
54	//axhos_step
55	. *Identify Pending Hallmarks (Pass 1)*:
56	.. Iterate `«PAIRS»` in sort order; each contiguous run of the same hallmark is one
57	{rbtga_hallmark}'s
58	ark inventory.
59	.. For each hallmark, record whether
60	{rbtga_ark_image},
61	{rbtga_ark_about},
62	and
63	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
64	each appear in its run.
65	.. A hallmark is *pending* when `image` and `about` are present and `vouch` is absent.
66	Collect pending hallmarks into `«PENDING»` (in-memory sequence).
67	.. If `«PENDING»` is empty, the procedure completes successfully with no vouches submitted.
68	
69	//axhos_step
70	. *Resolve Vessel and Vouch Per Pending Hallmark (Pass 2)*:
71	.. For each `«HALLMARK»` in `«PENDING»`:
72	... Extract the hallmark's
73	{rbtga_ark_about}
74	artifact (package `«HALLMARKS_ROOT»/«HALLMARK»/about`, tag `«HALLMARK»`) to a scratch directory via the shared GAR artifact extraction helper
75	... Read `.vessel_name` from the extracted `build_info.json` via `jq`
76	... {rbbc_store}
77	`«VESSEL_DIR»` as `«RBRR_VESSEL_DIR»/«VESSEL_NAME»`
78	... {rbbc_require}
79	`«VESSEL_DIR»` exists on disk — the vessel named in `about` metadata must correspond to a configured vessel in the current regime
80	... {rbbc_fatal}
81	if the `about` artifact cannot be extracted, `build_info.json` is missing, `.vessel_name` is empty, or `«VESSEL_DIR»` does not exist — the `about` artifact is the sole source of vessel identity for this batch; a gap there is a fail-fast stop
82	... Invoke
83	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
84	with `«VESSEL_DIR»` and `«HALLMARK»`.
85	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
86	loads the vessel's
87	{rbrv_vessel_mode}
88	internally and dispatches the appropriate verification (conjure: SLSA DSSE verification; bind: digest-pin; graft: GRAFTED stamp).
89	... {rbbc_fatal}
90	if
91	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
92	fails — the batch terminates at the broken hallmark.
93	
94	//axhoot_typed_output
95	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
96	. *Display Summary*:
97	.. {rbbc_show}
98	`Batch vouch complete — N/N hallmark(s) vouched` where N is the initial pending count
99	
100	//axhoc_completion
101	Successful execution creates one
102	{rbtga_ark_vouch}
103	artifact per pending hallmark (N total, where N = initial pending count).
104	The batch runs sequentially; a failure at any hallmark halts the remainder.
105	+
106	*Scope of vessel resolution*: Reading `vessel_name` from `build_info.json` is a narrow anticipation of the broader vessel-from-metadata pattern (AAL territory) — applied locally inside batch vouch only, because
107	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
108	's signature still takes a vessel directory.
109	Other operations (tally, plumb, abjure) continue to not surface vessel.
110	+
111	*Two-pass shape*: Pass 1 collects pending hallmarks into memory before Pass 2 begins processing.
112	Each
113	{rbtgo_ark_vouch}
114	invocation sources credentials and issues Cloud Build calls that would silently consume loop stdin if the pending set were streamed; the in-memory collection decouples enumeration from dispatch.
115	+
116	*Idempotency*: Re-running batch vouch after a successful batch finds an empty pending set (every prior pending hallmark now has `vouch` present) and exits with no action.
117	+
118	*No mode pre-check*: Earlier shapes of this operation pre-probed SLSA provenance for conjure-mode hallmarks to skip-with-warning those lacking provenance.
119	That pattern is gone — all mode-specific verification happens inside
120	{rbtgo_ark_vouch},
121	and any failure there surfaces as a fail-fast stop rather than being absorbed into a summary count.
122

input.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_conscript_powershell}

NOTE: Creates an ephemeral workload OS user on a Windows node with a
PowerShell shell, and provisions a corresponding workload
{burn_regime} profile locally.
Uses a named privileged BURN as authority for the remote-user
creation.
The PowerShell shell choice is independent of the privileged BURN's
shell.

//axhoq_precondition
A privileged-tier {burn_regime} profile exists locally and is
{bust_knock}-responsive against the target node.

//axhoq_precondition
The OpenSSH default shell on the target node is set to (or settable
to) `powershell.exe` for the workload account.

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate via privileged BURN*: open an authenticated session to
the target node using the privileged profile

//axhos_step
. *Create workload user*: provision a fresh OS user with PowerShell as
the login shell, and capture the temporary credential or pubkey trust
as defined by the platform ceremony

//axhos_step
. *Write workload profile*: persist a workload {burn_regime} profile
locally with `{burn_tier} = workload`, {burn_command} naming the
PowerShell launcher, and a reference to the privileged authority used

//axhos_step
. *Probe new account*: confirm passwordless SSH to the workload account
succeeds via {bust_knock}

//axhog_guarantee
A new workload OS user exists on the remote node with PowerShell as the
login shell, a corresponding workload {burn_regime} profile is
persisted locally, and the new account is reachable over passwordless
SSH.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when remote-user creation, local profile write, and verification
probe all succeed.
Non-zero on any of: privileged-BURN refusal, remote-user creation
failure, shell-association failure, local profile write failure, or
final probe failure.

content.content: Updated task #3 status

message.content: notch now.  Remind me what burn_alias is?  That doesn't seem right.

params.intent: draft BUSTxx detail-site subdocs for 15 Windows-relevant BURN tabtargets; wire includes into BUS0; preconditions/steps/guarantees/completion per AXLA axho* hierarchy; 21 voicings unchanged; buw-st passes

content.text:
Notched (e4c34615).

**`burn_alias` per BUS0 lines 939-943:**

```
[[burn_alias]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_alias}::
SSH alias and key filename.
Must match the directory name containing this profile's assignment file.
```

Voicing: `axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname`. So `burn_alias` is **a field inside the BURN regime** — one of the assignment lines in a profile file. It's the SSH alias AND the key filename AND (by constraint) must equal the directory name holding the profile.

**The concern you're right to flag.**

In my subdocs I wrote things like *"identified by a positional `{burn_alias}` argument"* and *"the supplied `{burn_alias}`"*. That conflates the **regime variable** (the field in the profile) with the **CLI positional argument** (the lookup key the operator types).

AXLA's `axhopt_typed_parameter` wants a *type term*. `axhopa_arg_parameter` wants a *shared CLI-arg term*. `{burn_alias}` is neither — it's an `axvr_variable`. I deliberately skipped the parameter markers because of this; but I still referenced `{burn_alias}` *in prose* as if it were the arg name. Same conflation, just hidden in NOTE bodies and step prose.

**Three ways to fix, in order of cost:**

1. **Prose-only fix**: change phrases like "positional `{burn_alias}` argument" to "positional alias argument" — describe the arg by role, not by quoin. The regime variable `{burn_alias}` is then mentioned only where it's actually the regime field (e.g., "the `{burn_alias}` field of the written profile must match the directory name"). Cheapest. No new minting. Maybe loses some link-ability.

2. **Mint a shared CLI-arg quoin** (`bust_alias_arg` or `busn_alias_input` or similar) in BUS0's mapping section and use `axhopa_arg_parameter` with it across the subdocs. Cleanest semantically. But the docket explicitly says "no new markers minted at the detail site" — that constrains hierarchy markers, not CLI-arg quoins. Adding a quoin in BUS0 might be in-scope or might be next-pace work.

3. **Accept the conflation** as load-bearing convenience — the alias-as-value and alias-as-field name share an identity by spec ("must match the directory name"). Document the dual-role once at `burn_alias`'s definition site and stop worrying. Cheapest if the convention is OK.

My read: option 1 is the right immediate fix — pure prose cleanup across the 15 subdocs, no new minting, faithful to AXLA's distinction between regime variable and CLI argument. If you want option 2 it's a separate slate-worthy unit (mint quoin + use it everywhere).

Which way do you want to go?

message.content: Ah I was confused.  burn_alias is fine as is.  Anything else to consider before we wrap?

content.text:
Three things worth flagging before wrap:

**1. Parameter markers were punted to prose.**
None of the 15 use `axhopt`/`axhopa`/`axhopm`. I justified this on the grounds that no shared CLI-arg vocabulary exists in BUS0 yet and `{burn_alias}` is a regime variable. The docket goal said "elaborating parameters, preconditions, and guarantees" — I did preconditions and guarantees with markers but parameters with prose only. AXLA treats parameter markers as optional, so the subdocs are spec-conformant, but a future pace that mints a shared CLI-arg quoin (or decides regime-variable references are acceptable for `axhopt`) would re-touch all 15.

**2. The BUST mnemonic legend isn't documented anywhere.**
The X/Y table I proposed (L=Lifecycle, G=Garrison, … / L=list, R=render, V=validate, …) lives only in this conversation and the git history of the notch. CLAUDE.md's *Quoin Sub-Letter Discipline* asks for the legend to live "in a comment block at the top of the spec's mapping section" — though that rule is scoped to quoin prefixes, not doc-namespace mnemonics. If a future pace adds a 16th BUST subdoc, the next minter has to re-derive. Two reasonable resolutions: (a) add a small comment block above the BUST includes in BUS0 documenting the X/Y assignment, or (b) leave it and trust the next minter to read existing filenames. Lightweight enough that (a) is probably worth doing now.

**3. `bust_handbook_node_top` is a thin fit.**
Its subdoc has 1 step, no precondition, and is essentially "render handbook content." Operations do work; this is a passthrough surface. The voicing is legitimately `axvo_tabtarget` so `axhob_operation` accepts it, but the shape is a stretch compared to its 14 siblings. Could be left as drafted — or in a future polish pace, this one might convert to a documentation-cross-reference rather than an operation block.

My recommendation: address **(2) now** with a small comment block in BUS0 (it's the only one that has lasting cost and is cheap to fix). Leave **(1)** and **(3)** as observations for future paces.

Want me to add the legend comment, or wrap as-is?

lastPrompt: Ah I was confused.  burn_alias is fine as is.  Anything else to consider before we wrap?

message.content:
1) make a list of the ones that have parameters- we do need to specify these correctly.
2) Your failure to trust existing document patterns leads you to keep re-proposing locally interesting ideas that would in the long term become word cancers.  I again reject this sub-table
3) Yes, it is deeply underspecified; in the fullness of time, it documents all node access tabtargets.  Easier to correct later.

content.text:
Acknowledged on (2) — point taken. No more local infrastructure proposals.

**Parameter inventory (per BUS0 catalog prose):**

| Tabtarget | Positional args |
|-----------|----------------|
| bust_node_regime_list | none |
| bust_node_regime_render | 1 — alias of profile to render |
| bust_node_regime_validate | 1 — alias of profile to validate |
| bust_garrison_cygwin | 1 — alias of privileged profile to establish |
| bust_garrison_wsl | 1 — alias of privileged profile to establish |
| bust_garrison_powershell | 1 — alias of privileged profile to establish |
| bust_conscript_cygwin | 1 — alias of privileged BURN (authority) |
| bust_conscript_wsl | 1 — alias of privileged BURN (authority) |
| bust_conscript_powershell | 1 — alias of privileged BURN (authority) |
| bust_discharge | 1 — alias of workload BURN to discharge |
| bust_inventory | 1 — alias of privileged BURN to query through |
| bust_knock | 1 — alias of profile to probe |
| bust_remote_run | 2 — alias of workload BURN, plus a command file path |
| bust_interactive_session | 1 — alias of workload BURN |
| bust_handbook_node_top | none |

So 13 of 15 take an alias arg; one of those (`bust_remote_run`) also takes a command-file path; two are zero-arg.

**Marker question.** The 13 alias args resolve naturally to `{burn_alias}` — that quoin already exists, is voiced `axvr_variable`, and you've confirmed it's the right reference. Existing RBK precedent (RBSAA-ark_abjure) uses `axhopt_typed_parameter` with attribute references that are voiced as concept entities rather than strict `axt_*` types — i.e., the corpus already treats `axhopt` as "the named input is *that* defined thing," not "the named input has *that* type." Applying that same shape here yields:

```
//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burn_alias}
```

…in each subdoc that takes the alias arg. For `bust_remote_run`, the second arg (command file) has no existing quoin to reference. Existing dispatch-side `{burd_target}` is dispatch-state, not the input. Two ways: stretch and use `{burd_target}` (it's the closest existing thing — "BURD_TARGET"), or describe the file path in prose without a marker. I'd lean toward prose for the command file unless you'd prefer I use `{burd_target}`.

Want me to apply `axhopt_typed_parameter {burn_alias}` to the 13 alias-taking subdocs, and you choose what to do for the second `remote_run` arg?

message.content: Do not use burd_target.  Make a quoin for a 'scribed command file' and use that.  Then yes add burn_alias as you suggest to all appropriate subdocs.  Use concurrent sonnet or haiku agents to do this fast in parallel, then check work after.

content.text: I'll mint the quoin myself first (sequential dependency), then dispatch parallel agents for the 13 subdoc edits.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_interactive_session}
3	
4	NOTE: Opens an interactive SSH session to the workload account named
5	by a workload {burn_regime} profile.
6	Foreground operation; control returns to the station shell when the
7	operator exits the remote shell.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burn_alias}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	A workload-tier {burn_regime} profile exists at the given {burn_alias}
14	and is currently {bust_knock}-responsive.
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Hand control to SSH*: invoke the local SSH client interactively
18	against the workload account
19

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_remote_run}
3	
4	NOTE: Executes the contents of a local command file on the remote node
5	and captures stdout, stderr, and the remote exit status to
6	{burd_output_dir}.
7	Workload-tier dispatch.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burn_alias}
11	
12	//axhopt_typed_parameter
13	{busi_scribed_command_file}
14	
15	//axhoq_precondition
16	A workload-tier {burn_regime} profile exists at the given {burn_alias}
17	and is currently {bust_knock}-responsive.
18	
19	//axhoq_precondition
20	The local command file referenced as the dispatch input exists and is
21	readable by the calling user.
22

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_knock}
3	
4	NOTE: Probes workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.
5	Used to confirm that a workload {burn_regime} profile is currently
6	usable before dispatching real work, and used internally by the
7	garrison and conscript ceremonies as their final verification step.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burn_alias}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	A {burn_regime} profile exists at the given {burn_alias} and references
14	a {burn_key_file} readable by the calling {burs_user}.
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Open SSH connection*: invoke the SSH client non-interactively using
18	the profile's {burn_alias} and {burn_key_file}
19

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_inventory}
3	
4	NOTE: Reports workload users currently provisioned on a node.
5	Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not just the
6	calling station's history.
7	Queries the node via the named privileged BURN.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burn_alias}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	A {burn_regime} profile with `{burn_tier} = privileged` exists at the
14	given {burn_alias} and authenticates without prompting.
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Authenticate via privileged BURN*: open an authenticated session to
18	the target node using the privileged profile
19

2	{bust_discharge}
3	
4	NOTE: Removes a workload user from a node and deletes the local
5	workload {burn_regime} profile.
6	The workload profile encodes its privileged authority; {bust_discharge}
7	uses that authority to delete the remote OS user, then unlinks the
8	local profile file.
9	
10	//axhopt_typed_parameter
11	{burn_alias}
12	
13	//axhoq_precondition
14	A {burn_regime} profile with `{burn_tier} = workload` exists at the
15	given {burn_alias} and references a privileged authority that is
16	currently {bust_knock}-responsive.
17	
18	//axhos_step
19	. *Resolve authority*: read the workload profile and resolve its
20	referenced privileged authority

Next: burh-to-burn-rename-buk (₢A-AAV) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  Mechanical rename + targeted deletion. Absorbs former AAU (tier-refusal helper). Replaces the former AAb colophon move with a clean deletion — the SSH config aggregator is dropped entirely because its premise is abandoned: `~/.ssh/config` is the user's concern, not BUK's, and the aggregator was never in BUS0.
  
  ## Docket
  
  **1. BURH→BURN rename across BUK.** Variables, function prefixes, module filenames, profile filename (`burh.env` → `burn.env`), zipper enrollments, tabtarget filenames. Constructor enrollments (`BUWZ_RHC_*`) and Windows command enrollments (`BUWZ_WC_*`) keep names provisionally — they retire in Garrison/Conscript paces, not here.
  
  **2. Add `BURN_TIER` enrollment** (`privileged|workload`).
  
  **3. Tier-refusal helper.** Mint `burn_assert_tier <expected> <alias>` — compares the alias's `BURN_TIER` to expected, exits non-zero before any side effect. Error names expected tier, observed tier, alias name. BUS0 already states the contract on `burn_tier`; this codifies the helper shape so Garrison/Conscript paces consume one implementation.
  
  **4. Delete the SSH config aggregator entirely.** No rename, no shim, no fallback:
  - `tt/buw-HWsc.SshConfig.sh` (the tabtarget file)
  - `burh_ssh_config()` function in `burh_cli.sh`
  - `BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG` enrollment in `buwz_zipper.sh`
  - "SSH Automation:" section header + `Write SSH config:` line in `buhw_top` (`buhw_windows.sh`)
  - Permission entries for `buw-HWsc.SshConfig.sh` in `.claude/settings.local.json`
  
  **5. Trim `~/.ssh/config` teaching from the handbook.** No fallbacks left behind:
  - Delete the "Create SSH Config Entry" block inside `buhw_access_remote` (`buhw_windows.sh`) — managing `~/.ssh/config` is not BUK's concern
  - Replace the bare `ssh ${z_alias}` verification step with `${BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH} <alias>` — no `ssh -i ...` fallback
  - Rename menu label "SSH client key & host config" → "SSH client key generation" in `buhw_windows.sh` (`buhw_top`) and `rbhw0_top.sh`
  - Update zipper description "Client key gen + ssh config" → "Client key generation"
  
  **Validation:** `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and BUK-touching fixtures pass.
  
  Post-condition: `grep -rn 'HWsc\|burh_ssh_config\|BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG' Tools/ tt/` returns nothing.
  
  **Discovery recipes:**
  - BURH rename: `grep -rln 'BURH\|burh' Tools/buk/ tt/`
  - Aggregator residue: `grep -rn 'HWsc\|burh_ssh_config\|BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG' Tools/ tt/`
  - Handbook ssh-config teaching: `grep -rn '\.ssh/config\|SSH config' Tools/buk/ Tools/rbk/`

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.
7	
8	## Current Design
9	
10	The architectural backbone lives in BUS0 §Remote Node Access (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`)
11	plus three platform subdocs (`BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc`, `BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc`,
12	`BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc`). Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock
13	captures only state that doesn't fit there.
14	
15	**Header decisions made in this heat:**
16	
17	- BURH regime renamed to BURN (n = Node) — decision; execution in AAV/AAW
18	- BURN_TIER field added: `privileged | workload`
19	- Two-tier model:
20	  - Privileged tier: node-level ops (sshd config, user provisioning).
21	    Multi-resident allowed; one operator at a time by social contract.
22	  - Workload tier: user-level ops (normal remote work). Exclusive to one
23	    station user; ephemeral.
24	- Verb vocabulary (BUSN quoins): Garrison / Conscript / Discharge / Inventory
25	- Tabtarget colophon families under `buw-`:
26	  - `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops (list / render / validate)
27	  - `buw-rnc` — SSH config aggregator (cross-tier, all profiles)
28	  - `buw-npg{c,w,p}` — Garrison per Windows shell (Cygwin/WSL/PowerShell)
29	  - `buw-nwc{c,w,p}` — Conscript per Windows shell
30	  - `buw-nw{d,i}` — Discharge / Inventory (Windows-side)
31	  - `buw-nw{k,r,s}` — Knock / Run / Ssh (workload operational)
32	  - `buw-hn0` — Handbook landing for node ops
33	- Workload responsiveness "knock" colophon is `buw-nwk` (originally
34	  `buw-nwc`; renamed to remove visual collision with conscript family
35	  `buw-nwc{platform}`).
36	- Linux/Mac/Localhost garrison and conscript variants (`buw-npg{l,m,x}`,
37	  `buw-nwc{l,m,x}`) are not slated and not itched. Mint as new paces when
38	  a first non-Windows fundus target actually arrives. Legacy `buw-rhc{l,m,x}`
39	  (BURN-renamed in AAV) survives as transitional code; localhost remains
40	  exercised by AAc and AAP regression paces.
41	- AAQ (BUSNW elaboration) dropped: BUS0 backbone is sufficient spec; per-
42	  platform contracts crystallize through implementation paces and AAC
43	  practice. BUSNW stub (36 lines) stands as-is.
44	
45	## Heat Sequence
46	
47	**Implementation phase (sequenced):**
48	
49	- ₢A-AAV burh-to-burn-rename-buk — mechanical rename + tier-refusal helper
50	  + SSH config aggregator move (`buw-HWsc` → `buw-rnc`)
51	- ₢A-AAW burh-to-burn-rename-jjk — mechanical, depends on AAV
52	- ₢A-AAX garrison-windows-machinery — three Windows shells; defines key-
53	  line wire format (`# BURN:<alias>` marker) inline; updates BUS0 catalog
54	- ₢A-AAY conscript-discharge-inventory-windows — Windows workload-tier
55	  mirror, depends on AAX; updates BUS0 catalog
56	- ₢A-AAZ workload-operational-tabtargets — Check / Run / Ssh
57	- ₢A-AAa node-handbook-landing-and-windows-residue — handbook restructure,
58	  depends on AAX and AAY
59	
60	**Curia smoke (FIRST testing — runs on Mac before any Windows trip):**
61	
62	- ₢A-AAc localhost-smoke-pre-windows — fundus scenario test on Mac;
63	  validates BURN rename + Windows-only scope didn't break localhost path
64	
65	**Practice phase (Windows host):**
66	
67	- ₢A-AAC practice-access-procedures — Garrison/Conscript on real Windows;
68	  pass criteria specified per shell variant in pace docket
69	- ₢A-AAD practice-environment-procedures — WSL + Cygwin install
70	- ₢A-AAE practice-fundus-provisioning — JJK fundus inside WSL
71	- ₢A-AAF practice-docker-procedures — Docker dual-daemon
72	
73	**Final regression:**
74	
75	- ₢A-AAP retest-fundus-after-practice — re-run localhost fundus scenario
76	  test after Windows practice has potentially amended code
77	
78	## Deferred (named for future return)
79	
80	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged
81	  trust. Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their
82	  own machine.
83	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin
84	  scenarios. Pairs naturally with Adopt when both are needed.
85	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't
86	  duplicate.
87	
88	## Standing Notes
89	
90	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as
91	  `100.x.y.z` / tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency
92	- Windows username `b hyslop` (with space) — audit shell quoting at
93	  every `${BURN_USER}` expansion during practice
94	- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`
95	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is
96	  the Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call
97	- BURS_USER station regime field routes profiles to
98	  `.buk/users/${BURS_USER}/` subdirectory
99	- Three Windows BURN profiles for `rocket` distinguished by

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.

## Current Design

The architectural backbone lives in BUS0 §Remote Node Access (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`)
plus three platform subdocs (`BUSNW-NodeWindows.adoc`, `BUSNL-NodeLinux.adoc`,
`BUSNM-NodeMac.adoc`). Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock
captures only state that doesn't fit there.

**Header decisions made in this heat:**

- BURH regime renamed to BURN (n = Node) — decision; execution in AAV/AAW
- BURN_TIER field added: `privileged | workload`
- Two-tier model:
  - Privileged tier: node-level ops (sshd config, user provisioning).
    Multi-resident allowed; one operator at a time by social contract.
  - Workload tier: user-level ops (normal remote work). Exclusive to one
    station user; ephemeral.
- Verb vocabulary (BUSN quoins): Garrison / Conscript / Discharge / Inventory
- Tabtarget colophon families under `buw-`:
  - `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops (list / render / validate)
  - `buw-rnc` — SSH config aggregator (cross-tier, all profiles)
  - `buw-npg{c,w,p}` — Garrison per Windows shell (Cygwin/WSL/PowerShell)
  - `buw-nwc{c,w,p}` — Conscript per Windows shell
  - `buw-nw{d,i}` — Discharge / Inventory (Windows-side)
  - `buw-nw{k,r,s}` — Knock / Run / Ssh (workload operational)
  - `buw-hn0` — Handbook landing for node ops
- Workload responsiveness "knock" colophon is `buw-nwk` (originally
  `buw-nwc`; renamed to remove visual collision with conscript family
  `buw-nwc{platform}`).
- Linux/Mac/Localhost garrison and conscript variants (`buw-npg{l,m,x}`,
  `buw-nwc{l,m,x}`) are not slated and not itched. Mint as new paces when
  a first non-Windows fundus target actually arrives. Legacy `buw-rhc{l,m,x}`
  (BURN-renamed in AAV) survives as transitional code; localhost remains
  exercised by AAc and AAP regression paces.
- AAQ (BUSNW elaboration) dropped: BUS0 backbone is sufficient spec; per-
  platform contracts crystallize through implementation paces and AAC
  practice. BUSNW stub (36 lines) stands as-is.

## Heat Sequence

**Implementation phase (sequenced):**

- ₢A-AAV burh-to-burn-rename-buk — mechanical rename + tier-refusal helper
  + SSH config aggregator move (`buw-HWsc` → `buw-rnc`)
- ₢A-AAW burh-to-burn-rename-jjk — mechanical, depends on AAV
- ₢A-AAX garrison-windows-machinery — three Windows shells; defines key-
  line wire format (`# BURN:<alias>` marker) inline; updates BUS0 catalog
- ₢A-AAY conscript-discharge-inventory-windows — Windows workload-tier
  mirror, depends on AAX; updates BUS0 catalog
- ₢A-AAZ workload-operational-tabtargets — Check / Run / Ssh
- ₢A-AAa node-handbook-landing-and-windows-residue — handbook restructure,
  depends on AAX and AAY

**Curia smoke (FIRST testing — runs on Mac before any Windows trip):**

- ₢A-AAc localhost-smoke-pre-windows — fundus scenario test on Mac;
  validates BURN rename + Windows-only scope didn't break localhost path

**Practice phase (Windows host):**

- ₢A-AAC practice-access-procedures — Garrison/Conscript on real Windows;
  pass criteria specified per shell variant in pace docket
- ₢A-AAD practice-environment-procedures — WSL + Cygwin install
- ₢A-AAE practice-fundus-provisioning — JJK fundus inside WSL
- ₢A-AAF practice-docker-procedures — Docker dual-daemon

**Final regression:**

- ₢A-AAP retest-fundus-after-practice — re-run localhost fundus scenario
  test after Windows practice has potentially amended code

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged
  trust. Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their
  own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin
  scenarios. Pairs naturally with Adopt when both are needed.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't
  duplicate.

## Standing Notes

- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as
  `100.x.y.z` / tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency
- Windows username `b hyslop` (with space) — audit shell quoting at
  every `${BURN_USER}` expansion during practice
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is
  the Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call
- BURS_USER station regime field routes profiles to
  `.buk/users/${BURS_USER}/` subdirectory
- Three Windows BURN profiles for `rocket` distinguished by
  `BURN_COMMAND`: cygwin (`...bash.exe -l`), WSL (`wsl.exe -d ...`),
  PowerShell (`powershell.exe`)

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAV

## Character
Mechanical rename + targeted deletion. Absorbs former AAU (tier-refusal helper). Replaces the former AAb colophon move with a clean deletion — the SSH config aggregator is dropped entirely because its premise is abandoned: `~/.ssh/config` is the user's concern, not BUK's, and the aggregator was never in BUS0.

## Docket

**1. BURH→BURN rename across BUK.** Variables, function prefixes, module filenames, profile filename (`burh.env` → `burn.env`), zipper enrollments, tabtarget filenames. Constructor enrollments (`BUWZ_RHC_*`) and Windows command enrollments (`BUWZ_WC_*`) keep names provisionally — they retire in Garrison/Conscript paces, not here.

**2. Add `BURN_TIER` enrollment** (`privileged|workload`).

**3. Tier-refusal helper.** Mint `burn_assert_tier <expected> <alias>` — compares the alias's `BURN_TIER` to expected, exits non-zero before any side effect. Error names expected tier, observed tier, alias name. BUS0 already states the contract on `burn_tier`; this codifies the helper shape so Garrison/Conscript paces consume one implementation.

**4. Delete the SSH config aggregator entirely.** No rename, no shim, no fallback:
- `tt/buw-HWsc.SshConfig.sh` (the tabtarget file)
- `burh_ssh_config()` function in `burh_cli.sh`
- `BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG` enrollment in `buwz_zipper.sh`
- "SSH Automation:" section header + `Write SSH config:` line in `buhw_top` (`buhw_windows.sh`)
- Permission entries for `buw-HWsc.SshConfig.sh` in `.claude/settings.local.json`

**5. Trim `~/.ssh/config` teaching from the handbook.** No fallbacks left behind:
- Delete the "Create SSH Config Entry" block inside `buhw_access_remote` (`buhw_windows.sh`) — managing `~/.ssh/config` is not BUK's concern
- Replace the bare `ssh ${z_alias}` verification step with `${BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH} <alias>` — no `ssh -i ...` fallback
- Rename menu label "SSH client key & host config" → "SSH client key generation" in `buhw_windows.sh` (`buhw_top`) and `rbhw0_top.sh`
- Update zipper description "Client key gen + ssh config" → "Client key generation"

**Validation:** `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and BUK-touching fixtures pass.

Post-condition: `grep -rn 'HWsc\|burh_ssh_config\|BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG' Tools/ tt/` returns nothing.

**Discovery recipes:**
- BURH rename: `grep -rln 'BURH\|burh' Tools/buk/ tt/`
- Aggregator residue: `grep -rn 'HWsc\|burh_ssh_config\|BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG' Tools/ tt/`
- Handbook ssh-config teaching: `grep -rn '\.ssh/config\|SSH config' Tools/buk/ Tools/rbk/`

lastPrompt: mount A-

aiTitle: Find AXLA template with hierarchy markers

content.text:
Mounted. Heat ₣A- now 9/21 done; AAd just wrapped. Next pace is **burh-to-burn-rename-buk (₢A-AAV)** — the first implementation pace per the heat sequence.

**Character**: Mechanical rename + targeted deletion. Substantial scope across BUK kit.

**Five docketed deltas**:
1. **BURH→BURN rename** across BUK (variables, function prefixes, module filenames, profile filename `burh.env`→`burn.env`, zipper enrollments, tabtarget filenames). Provisionally exempt: `BUWZ_RHC_*` constructor enrollments and `BUWZ_WC_*` Windows command enrollments — those retire in later paces, not here.
2. **Add `BURN_TIER` enrollment** (`privileged|workload`).
3. **Mint `burn_assert_tier <expected> <alias>` helper** — compares alias's `BURN_TIER` to expected, exits non-zero before side effects with a diagnostic that names expected/observed tier and alias.
4. **Delete the SSH config aggregator** — five concrete sites listed (tabtarget file, function, enrollment, handbook section, settings permission).
5. **Trim `~/.ssh/config` teaching from handbook** — four sub-edits (delete the config-entry block, swap bare ssh for `BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH`, rename two menu labels, update zipper description).

**Gate**: `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` + BUK-touching fixtures pass; `grep -rn 'HWsc\|burh_ssh_config\|BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG' Tools/ tt/` returns empty.

**Name assessment**: "burh-to-burn-rename-buk" fits — covers the rename core; "rename" reasonably encompasses the targeted-deletion riders since they all flow from the same regime-renaming. I'd keep it.

**Proposed approach** (sequential phases):
1. **Discovery** — run all three recipes from the docket; build full site list before editing anything
2. **Phase 1 (rename)** — files first (`burh_cli.sh`/`burh_regime.sh`/`burh.env` → `burn_*`), then content edits across affected files (functions, vars, refs); rename `buw-rh{l,r,v}.*.sh` tabtargets to `buw-rn{l,r,v}.*.sh` per paddock decision
3. **Phase 2 (BURN_TIER)** — add field to renamed `burn.env` template + regime kindling
4. **Phase 3 (assert-tier helper)** — add `burn_assert_tier` to `burn_regime.sh`
5. **Phase 4 (aggregator deletion)** — five sites, sequential deletes
6. **Phase 5 (handbook trim)** — four sub-edits
7. **Validation** — run self-test, verify post-condition grep returns empty

**Strategy**: Sequential **sonnet**. Mechanical but the rename has cross-file coordination — running parallel agents on a code rename invites "agent A renamed file but agent B still references old name" hazards. Phase 1 alone is large enough to reason about file-by-file; phases 2-5 are local.

Tooling note: bash work, no Rust; build discipline doesn't apply. Self-test is the gate.

Want me to start with discovery before committing to anything else? (Discovery first lets us confirm site count and revisit phase ordering if the surface is bigger than the docket suggests.)

content.text:
Discovery complete. Bigger surface than the docket recipes alone surface — flagging additions below.

**Discovery summary:**

| Layer | Sites |
|-------|-------|
| Module content (BURH→BURN) | 6 files, 144 hits — `burh_cli.sh` (74), `burh_regime.sh` (39), `buwz_zipper.sh` (17), `buwc_cli.sh` (6), `buwc_windows.sh` (6), `buhw_windows.sh` (2) |
| Module file renames | 2 — `burh_cli.sh`→`burn_cli.sh`, `burh_regime.sh`→`burn_regime.sh` |
| Tabtarget renames | 3 — `buw-rh{l,r,v}.*.sh` → `buw-rn{l,r,v}.*.sh` |
| **Tracked profile files** | **7 — `.buk/users/bhyslop/{jjfu-full,jjfu-nogit,jjfu-nokey,jjfu-norepo,winhost-cyg,winhost-ps,winhost-wsl}/burh.env`** |
| Aggregator deletion | 5 — `tt/buw-HWsc.SshConfig.sh` (whole file), `burh_ssh_config()` (burh_cli.sh:189), `BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG` (buwz_zipper.sh:86), handbook line (buhw_windows.sh:323), perms (.claude/settings.local.json:779,789) |
| Handbook trim sites | 5 — `buhw_windows.sh:128` (doc_brief), `:332` (menu label), `rbhw0_top.sh:41` (menu label), `buwz_zipper.sh:100` (description), and the "Create SSH Config Entry" block + bare-ssh swap inside `buhw_access_remote` |

**Three things the docket didn't enumerate but are real surface:**

1. **Seven tracked `burh.env` profile files** in `.buk/users/bhyslop/` — these are committed, contain `BURH_HOST`/`BURH_USER`/`BURH_ALIAS`/`BURH_SSH_PUBKEY`/`BURH_KEY_FILE`/`BURH_COMMAND` lines, and need (a) `git mv` to `burn.env`, (b) content rename to `BURN_*`, (c) new `BURN_TIER` field. The four `jjfu-*` profiles look like fundus test fixtures (probably `workload` tier); the three `winhost-*` profiles look like real Windows access (probably `privileged` for first-trust setup, but could be either).

2. **Tabtarget silks for the 3 renamed launchers** — current `ListHostProfiles`/`RenderHostProfile`/`ValidateHostProfile` use "Host" terminology. With BURN = Node, candidates: `ListNodeRegimes`/`RenderNodeRegime`/`ValidateNodeRegime` (matches the existing `rbw-rn*` family pattern of "NameplateRegime"). Or just keep "Host" since it's still describing the same concept. Your call.

3. **`.claude/settings.local.json` line 790** — a separate permission for a `find -exec grep "BURH_HOST\|BURH_USER\|..."` command. Not the buw-HWsc permission targeted by the docket; it's a search permission. Update the BURH_* identifiers to BURN_* alongside the rename, or leave?

**Three questions before I plan execution:**

a. **BURN_TIER assignment for existing profiles** — what tier do `jjfu-*` get (likely `workload`)? What about `winhost-cyg`/`winhost-ps`/`winhost-wsl` (likely `privileged` since they're the trust-establishment profiles, but could also be workload entries you've kept post-conscript)?

b. **New silks** for `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — `ListNodeRegimes`/`RenderNodeRegime`/`ValidateNodeRegime`, keep `Host*`, or something else?

c. **Settings line 790 grep permission** — update BURH_* to BURN_* in-place, or leave alone?

Once answered, I'll lay out the seven-phase execution plan and proceed.

- Settings line 790 BURH_* → BURN_* in-place

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Host Regime subsystem (burh_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burh_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_VALIDATE "buw-rhv" "${z_mod}" "burh_validate" "param1" "Validate BURH profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_RENDER   "buw-rhr" "${z_mod}" "burh_render"   "param1" "Render BURH profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_LIST     "buw-rhl" "${z_mod}" "burh_list"     ""       "List BURH profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"

  # Host Regime — constructors + SSH automation (burh_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burh_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LINUX      "buw-rhcl"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_linux"      ""        "Construct BURH profile: Linux"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_MAC        "buw-rhcm"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_mac"        ""        "Construct BURH profile: macOS"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_CYGWIN     "buw-rhcc"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_cygwin"     ""        "Construct BURH profile: Cygwin"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_WSL        "buw-rhcw"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_wsl"        ""        "Construct BURH profile: WSL"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_POWERSHELL "buw-rhcp"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_powershell" ""        "Construct BURH profile: PowerShell"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LOCALHOST  "buw-rhcx"  "${z_mod}" "burh_construct_localhost"  ""        "Construct BURH profile: localhost"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_SSH_CONFIG  "buw-HWsc"  "${z_mod}" "burh_ssh_config"           ""        "Write SSH config from BURH profiles"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH  "buw-HWvs"  "${z_mod}" "burh_verify_ssh"           "param1"  "Verify SSH to BURH profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RH_INSTALL_KEY "buw-rhk"   "${z_mod}" "burh_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURH key into authorized_keys"

  # Windows Commands (buwc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buwc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_BOOTSTRAP   "buw-wcb"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_bootstrap_sshd"       ""        "Provision sshd on Windows (WSL)"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_WC_INSTALL_KEY "buw-wck"   "${z_mod}" "buwc_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURH key into Windows admin authorized_keys"

  # Handbook — top index + Windows OS procedures (buhw_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhw_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_H0_TOP            "buw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_handbook_top"        ""        "BUK top-level handbook index"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_TOP            "buw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_top"                ""        "BUK-level Windows procedures checklist"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE    "buw-HWab"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_base"        ""        "OpenSSH server install + lockdown"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE  "buw-HWar"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_remote"      ""        "Client key gen + ssh config"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY   "buw-HWax"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_entrypoints" ""        "SSH command= routing + icacls"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL        "buw-HWew"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_wsl"    "param1"  "WSL distro creation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN     "buw-HWec"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_cygwin" ""        "Cygwin install"

  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zbuwz_healthcheck() {
  zbuwz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuwz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
}

# eof

.buk/users/README.md:8:.buk/users/<BURS_USER>/<alias>/burh.env
.buk/users/README.md:11:Each subdirectory under a user is a BURH host regime profile — one SSH connection configuration. The `<alias>` must match the `BURH_ALIAS` value inside the `burh.env` file.
.buk/users/README.md:13:`BURH_KEY_FILE` specifies the SSH private key filename in `~/.ssh/`. When empty, defaults to the alias name. Localhost profiles set `id_ed25519` to share the invoking user's default key.
.buk/users/README.md:17:BURH profiles contain **public** key material only (`BURH_SSH_PUBKEY`). Private keys are never stored here. These files are safe to commit.
.buk/users/README.md:22:.buk/users/bhyslop/winhost-cyg/burh.env   # SSH to Windows via Cygwin bash
.buk/users/README.md:23:.buk/users/bhyslop/winhost-wsl/burh.env   # SSH to Windows via WSL
.buk/users/README.md:24:.buk/users/bhyslop/winhost-ps/burh.env    # SSH to Windows via PowerShell

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
#
# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURN_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburn_*)

zburn_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURN

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_

  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURN

  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
}

zburn_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
zburn_enforce() {
  zburn_sentinel

  buv_vet BURN
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burn_*)

# Build a BURN authorized_keys line with alias marker.
# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
# Sets ZBURN_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURN_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
# Prerequisite: BURN kindled (needs BURN_ALIAS, BURN_COMMAND, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY)
zburn_build_key_line() {
  zburn_sentinel

  ZBURN_KEY_MARKER="# BURN:${BURN_ALIAS}"

  if test -n "${BURN_COMMAND}"; then
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURN_COMMAND}\" ${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  else
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  fi
}

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
# content; presence is the fact).
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burn.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# eof

content.subject: Phase 2: git mv profile files

content.subject: Phase 3: git mv tabtargets

content.subject: Phase 4: BURH→BURN content rename
content.description: Rename BURH_*→BURN_*, burh_*→burn_*, burh.env→burn.env across 6 source files + 7 profiles + 3 tabtargets + settings.local.json

content.subject: Phase 5: Add BURN_TIER field
content.description: Add BURN_TIER=workload to 7 burn.env profiles; add BURN_TIER variable to burn_regime.sh kindling

content.subject: Phase 6: Mint burn_assert_tier helper
content.description: Add burn_assert_tier <expected> <alias> function to burn_regime.sh

content.description: Delete Create SSH Config Entry block; swap bare ssh for VERIFY_SSH; rename 2 menu labels + zipper description + doc_brief

content.subject: Phase 9: Validation
content.description: Run tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh; verify post-condition grep returns empty

# Assert that the named alias's profile has the expected BURN_TIER value.
# Exits non-zero with diagnostic before any side effect on tier mismatch.
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_assert_tier() {
  local -r z_expected="${1:-}"
  local -r z_alias="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: expected tier required (privileged|workload)"
  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias required"

  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: profile not found for alias '${z_alias}': ${z_profile_file}"

  local z_observed
  z_observed=$(grep '^BURN_TIER=' "${z_profile_file}" | head -n1 | cut -d= -f2-)
  test -n "${z_observed}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: BURN_TIER missing or empty in profile for alias '${z_alias}'"

  test "${z_observed}" = "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias '${z_alias}' has BURN_TIER='${z_observed}', expected '${z_expected}'"
}

# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
#
# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURN_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburn_*)

zburn_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURN

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  buv_group_enroll "Privilege Tier"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_TIER  "Tier classification: privileged for node-level ops, workload for ephemeral remote work"  privileged  workload

  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_

  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURN

  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
}

zburn_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
zburn_enforce() {
  zburn_sentinel

  buv_vet BURN
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burn_*)

# Build a BURN authorized_keys line with alias marker.
# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
# Sets ZBURN_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURN_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
# Prerequisite: BURN kindled (needs BURN_ALIAS, BURN_COMMAND, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY)
zburn_build_key_line() {
  zburn_sentinel

  ZBURN_KEY_MARKER="# BURN:${BURN_ALIAS}"

  if test -n "${BURN_COMMAND}"; then
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURN_COMMAND}\" ${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  else
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  fi
}

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
# content; presence is the fact).
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burn.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# eof

+# Exits non-zero with diagnostic before any side effect on tier mismatch.
+  test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: profile not found for alias '${z_alias}': ${z_profile_file}"
+  test -n "${z_observed}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: BURN_TIER missing or empty in profile for alias '${z_alias}'"
+  test "${z_observed}" = "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias '${z_alias}' has BURN_TIER='${z_observed}', expected '${z_expected}'"

# Build a BURN authorized_keys line with alias marker.

lines: buv_string_enroll  BURN_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"
+  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_TIER  "Tier classification: privileged for node-level ops, workload for ephemeral remote work"  privileged  workload
# Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment

input.new_string:
test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# Assert that the named alias's profile has the expected BURN_TIER value.
# Exits non-zero with diagnostic before any side effect on tier mismatch.
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_assert_tier() {
  local -r z_expected="${1:-}"
  local -r z_alias="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: expected tier required (privileged|workload)"
  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias required"

  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: profile not found for alias '${z_alias}': ${z_profile_file}"

  local z_observed
  z_observed=$(grep '^BURN_TIER=' "${z_profile_file}" | head -n1 | cut -d= -f2-)
  test -n "${z_observed}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: BURN_TIER missing or empty in profile for alias '${z_alias}'"

  test "${z_observed}" = "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias '${z_alias}' has BURN_TIER='${z_observed}', expected '${z_expected}'"
}

# eof
